-- MMS TimescaleDB Configuration Migration
-- Configures hypertables, compression, retention, and continuous aggregates
--
-- Run order: 2 (after extensions, after schema creation)
-- Requires: timescaledb extension, mileage_readings table exists
-- @see specs/003-database-schema.md Section 3.5

-- =============================================================================
-- HYPERTABLE CONFIGURATION
-- =============================================================================

-- Convert mileage_readings to hypertable with 7-day chunks
-- Chunk interval chosen for:
-- - Optimal query performance for weekly/monthly reports
-- - Efficient compression (older chunks compress better)
-- - Balanced chunk count (~52 chunks/year vs 365 with daily)
SELECT create_hypertable(
    'mileage_readings',
    'reading_date',
    chunk_time_interval => INTERVAL '7 days',
    if_not_exists => TRUE,
    migrate_data => TRUE
);

-- Add index for common query patterns (train + date range)
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_mileage_train_date_hyper
    ON mileage_readings (train_id, reading_date DESC);

-- =============================================================================
-- COMPRESSION POLICY
-- =============================================================================

-- Enable compression on mileage_readings
-- Compresses chunks older than 30 days
-- Segmentation by train_id for efficient per-train queries on compressed data
ALTER TABLE mileage_readings SET (
    timescaledb.compress,
    timescaledb.compress_segmentby = 'train_id',
    timescaledb.compress_orderby = 'reading_date DESC'
);

-- Schedule automatic compression for chunks older than 30 days
SELECT add_compression_policy(
    'mileage_readings',
    INTERVAL '30 days',
    if_not_exists => TRUE
);

-- =============================================================================
-- RETENTION POLICY
-- =============================================================================

-- 13-year retention for government contract compliance
-- Contract is 12 years + 1 year buffer for audits
-- Note: Uses drop_chunks, not deletion - chunks are removed entirely
SELECT add_retention_policy(
    'mileage_readings',
    INTERVAL '13 years',
    if_not_exists => TRUE
);

-- =============================================================================
-- CONTINUOUS AGGREGATE: Daily Fleet Mileage
-- =============================================================================

-- Pre-computed daily mileage per fleet for dashboard performance
-- Refreshes automatically with real-time aggregation for recent data
CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW IF NOT EXISTS daily_fleet_mileage
WITH (timescaledb.continuous) AS
SELECT
    time_bucket('1 day', mr.reading_date) AS bucket_date,
    t.fleet_id,
    COUNT(DISTINCT mr.train_id) AS train_count,
    SUM(mr.daily_km) AS total_daily_km,
    AVG(mr.daily_km) AS avg_daily_km,
    MIN(mr.daily_km) AS min_daily_km,
    MAX(mr.daily_km) AS max_daily_km,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE mr.validation_status = 'VALID') AS valid_readings,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE mr.validation_status = 'ANOMALY') AS anomaly_readings,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE mr.validation_status = 'PENDING') AS pending_readings
FROM mileage_readings mr
JOIN trains t ON mr.train_id = t.id
WHERE mr.validation_status IN ('VALID', 'ANOMALY', 'PENDING')
GROUP BY time_bucket('1 day', mr.reading_date), t.fleet_id
WITH NO DATA;

-- Add real-time aggregation for recent data
-- Combines materialized results with live query for latest data
ALTER MATERIALIZED VIEW daily_fleet_mileage SET (
    timescaledb.materialized_only = FALSE
);

-- Refresh policy: runs daily, captures last 7 days
-- Overlapping refresh window ensures no gaps
SELECT add_continuous_aggregate_policy(
    'daily_fleet_mileage',
    start_offset => INTERVAL '7 days',
    end_offset => INTERVAL '1 hour',
    schedule_interval => INTERVAL '1 day',
    if_not_exists => TRUE
);

-- Index for fleet lookups
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_daily_fleet_mileage_fleet
    ON daily_fleet_mileage (fleet_id, bucket_date DESC);

-- =============================================================================
-- VERIFICATION
-- =============================================================================

DO $$
DECLARE
    hyper_count INTEGER;
    policy_count INTEGER;
BEGIN
    -- Verify hypertable exists
    SELECT COUNT(*) INTO hyper_count
    FROM timescaledb_information.hypertables
    WHERE hypertable_name = 'mileage_readings';

    IF hyper_count = 0 THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'mileage_readings hypertable not created';
    END IF;

    -- Verify compression policy exists
    SELECT COUNT(*) INTO policy_count
    FROM timescaledb_information.jobs
    WHERE hypertable_name = 'mileage_readings'
    AND proc_name = 'policy_compression';

    IF policy_count = 0 THEN
        RAISE WARNING 'Compression policy not found - may need manual creation';
    END IF;

    -- Verify retention policy exists
    SELECT COUNT(*) INTO policy_count
    FROM timescaledb_information.jobs
    WHERE hypertable_name = 'mileage_readings'
    AND proc_name = 'policy_retention';

    IF policy_count = 0 THEN
        RAISE WARNING 'Retention policy not found - may need manual creation';
    END IF;

    RAISE NOTICE 'TimescaleDB configuration verified successfully';
END $$;

1→-- MMS Database Functions & Triggers Migration
     2→-- Business logic implemented at database level for consistency
     3→--
     4→-- Run order: 3 (after schema creation)
     5→-- @see specs/003-database-schema.md Section 3.6
     6→
     7→-- =============================================================================
     8→-- HELPER FUNCTIONS
     9→-- =============================================================================
    10→
    11→-- updated_at trigger function (reusable)
    12→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION update_updated_at_column()
    13→RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
    14→BEGIN
    15→    NEW.updated_at = NOW();
    16→    RETURN NEW;
    17→END;
    18→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
    19→
    20→-- =============================================================================
    21→-- TRAIN FITNESS SYNC FROM UNAVAILABILITY EVENTS
    22→-- =============================================================================
    23→
    24→-- Automatically update train.fitness_status when unavailability events change
    25→-- Priority: GROUNDED > RESTRICTED > FIT
    26→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION sync_train_fitness_from_unavailability()
    27→RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
    28→DECLARE
    29→    has_grounded BOOLEAN;
    30→    has_restricted BOOLEAN;
    31→    new_status fitness_status;
    32→BEGIN
    33→    -- Determine train ID to update
    34→    -- Handle both INSERT/UPDATE (NEW) and DELETE (OLD)
    35→    DECLARE
    36→        target_train_id UUID := COALESCE(NEW.train_id, OLD.train_id);
    37→    BEGIN
    38→        -- Check for active GROUNDED events
    39→        SELECT EXISTS (
    40→            SELECT 1 FROM unavailability_events
    41→            WHERE train_id = target_train_id
    42→            AND status = 'ACTIVE'
    43→            AND restriction_level = 'GROUNDED'
    44→        ) INTO has_grounded;
    45→
    46→        -- Check for active RESTRICTED events
    47→        SELECT EXISTS (
    48→            SELECT 1 FROM unavailability_events
    49→            WHERE train_id = target_train_id
    50→            AND status = 'ACTIVE'
    51→            AND restriction_level = 'RESTRICTED'
    52→        ) INTO has_restricted;
    53→
    54→        -- Determine new fitness status (priority order)
    55→        IF has_grounded THEN
    56→            new_status := 'GROUNDED';
    57→        ELSIF has_restricted THEN
    58→            new_status := 'RESTRICTED';
    59→        ELSE
    60→            new_status := 'FIT';
    61→        END IF;
    62→
    63→        -- Update train only if status changed
    64→        UPDATE trains
    65→        SET fitness_status = new_status,
    66→            updated_at = NOW()
    67→        WHERE id = target_train_id
    68→        AND fitness_status IS DISTINCT FROM new_status;
    69→
    70→        RETURN COALESCE(NEW, OLD);
    71→    END;
    72→END;
    73→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
    74→
    75→-- Trigger for unavailability_events changes
    76→DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS trg_sync_train_fitness ON unavailability_events;

    86→-- Refresh all canvas-related materialized views concurrently

    89→RETURNS VOID AS $$
    90→BEGIN

    93→        SELECT 1 FROM pg_matviews WHERE matviewname = 'v_canvas_strategic'
    94→    ) THEN

   112→-- =============================================================================
   113→-- SCENARIO EXPIRATION CLEANUP
   114→-- =============================================================================
   115→
   116→-- Clean up expired scenarios (30-day inactivity default)
   117→-- Called by scheduled job (e.g., daily at 2 AM)
   118→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION cleanup_expired_scenarios()
   119→RETURNS INTEGER AS $$
   120→DECLARE
   121→    deleted_count INTEGER;
   122→BEGIN
   123→    -- Mark expired scenarios as EXPIRED
   124→    WITH expired AS (
   125→        UPDATE scenarios
   126→        SET status = 'EXPIRED',
   127→            updated_at = NOW()
   128→        WHERE status = 'ACTIVE'
   129→        AND expires_at < NOW()
   130→        RETURNING id
   131→    )
   132→    SELECT COUNT(*) INTO deleted_count FROM expired;
   133→
   134→    -- Delete scenario deltas for expired scenarios older than 90 days
   135→    -- (keep for audit trail for 90 days after expiration)
   136→    DELETE FROM scenario_deltas
   137→    WHERE scenario_id IN (
   138→        SELECT id FROM scenarios
   139→        WHERE status = 'EXPIRED'
   140→        AND expires_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '90 days'
   141→    );
   142→
   143→    -- Log cleanup action
   144→    IF deleted_count > 0 THEN
   145→        INSERT INTO audit_log (
   146→            entity_type, entity_id, action, details, created_at
   147→        ) VALUES (
   148→            'scenario',
   149→            '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000'::UUID,
   150→            'BULK_EXPIRE',
   151→            jsonb_build_object('expired_count', deleted_count),
   152→            NOW()
   153→        );
   154→    END IF;
   155→
   156→    RETURN deleted_count;
   157→END;
   158→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
   159→
   160→-- =============================================================================
   161→-- MATERIAL RESERVATION CLEANUP
   162→-- =============================================================================
   163→
   164→-- Release soft reservations that have expired
   165→-- Soft reservations: quantity_reserved at 90 days, convert to hard at 14 days
   166→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION release_expired_reservations()
   167→RETURNS INTEGER AS $$
   168→DECLARE
   169→    released_count INTEGER;
   170→BEGIN
   171→    -- Release SOFT reservations older than 90 days
   172→    WITH released AS (
   173→        UPDATE material_reservations
   174→        SET status = 'EXPIRED',
   175→            updated_at = NOW()
   176→        WHERE reservation_type = 'SOFT'
   177→        AND status = 'RESERVED'
   178→        AND created_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '90 days'
   179→        RETURNING id, inventory_position_id, quantity_reserved
   180→    ),
   181→    -- Update inventory positions to release reserved quantity
   182→    inventory_updates AS (
   183→        UPDATE inventory_positions ip
   184→        SET quantity_reserved = ip.quantity_reserved - r.quantity_reserved,
   185→            quantity_available = ip.quantity_available + r.quantity_reserved,
   186→            updated_at = NOW()
   187→        FROM released r
   188→        WHERE ip.id = r.inventory_position_id
   189→    )
   190→    SELECT COUNT(*) INTO released_count FROM released;
   191→
   192→    IF released_count > 0 THEN
   193→        RAISE NOTICE 'Released % expired soft reservations', released_count;
   194→    END IF;
   195→
   196→    RETURN released_count;
   197→END;
   198→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

   200→-- =============================================================================
   201→-- FLEET MILEAGE TARGET CALCULATION
   202→-- =============================================================================
   203→
   204→-- Recalculate target_mileage_km for all trains in a fleet
   205→-- Used for fleet balancing: equalizes mileage across trains
   206→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION update_fleet_mileage_targets(p_fleet_id UUID)
   207→RETURNS VOID AS $$
   208→DECLARE
   209→    fleet_avg_mileage NUMERIC;
   210→    fleet_total_target NUMERIC;
   211→    train_count INTEGER;
   212→    target_per_train NUMERIC;
   213→BEGIN
   214→    -- Get fleet statistics
   215→    SELECT
   216→        AVG(current_mileage_km),
   217→        SUM(current_mileage_km),
   218→        COUNT(*)
   219→    INTO fleet_avg_mileage, fleet_total_target, train_count
   220→    FROM trains
   221→    WHERE fleet_id = p_fleet_id
   222→    AND fitness_status != 'GROUNDED';
   223→
   224→    IF train_count = 0 THEN
   225→        RAISE NOTICE 'No active trains in fleet %', p_fleet_id;
   226→        RETURN;
   227→    END IF;
   228→
   229→    -- Calculate target: aim for equal distribution
   230→    -- Target = fleet average (equalizes wear across fleet)
   231→    target_per_train := ROUND(fleet_avg_mileage);
   232→
   233→    -- Update all trains in fleet
   234→    UPDATE trains
   235→    SET target_mileage_km = target_per_train,
   236→        updated_at = NOW()
   237→    WHERE fleet_id = p_fleet_id
   238→    AND fitness_status != 'GROUNDED';
   239→
   240→    RAISE NOTICE 'Updated % trains in fleet % with target mileage %',
   241→        train_count, p_fleet_id, target_per_train;
   242→END;
   243→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
   244→
   245→-- =============================================================================
   246→-- PM HIERARCHY SATISFACTION
   247→-- =============================================================================
   248→
   249→-- When a higher PM (e.g., C4) is completed, satisfy lower PMs (C1, C2, C3)
   250→-- This resets the lower PM counters as per PM hierarchy rules
   251→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION apply_pm_hierarchy_satisfaction()
   252→RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
   253→DECLARE
   254→    satisfied_codes TEXT[];
   255→    pm_code TEXT;
   256→BEGIN
   257→    -- Only trigger on PM completion
   258→    IF NEW.status != 'COMPLETED' OR OLD.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN
   259→        RETURN NEW;
   260→    END IF;
   261→
   262→    -- Get satisfies_codes for this PM type
   263→    SELECT pt.satisfies_codes, pt.pm_code
   264→    INTO satisfied_codes, pm_code
   265→    FROM pm_types pt
   266→    WHERE pt.id = NEW.pm_type_id;
   267→
   268→    IF satisfied_codes IS NULL OR array_length(satisfied_codes, 1) IS NULL THEN
   269→        RETURN NEW;
   270→    END IF;
   271→
   272→    -- Update pm_tracking for all satisfied PM types
   273→    UPDATE pm_tracking
   274→    SET last_pm_date = NEW.actual_completion_date,
   275→        last_pm_mileage_km = NEW.actual_mileage_km,
   276→        updated_at = NOW()
   277→    WHERE train_id = NEW.train_id
   278→    AND pm_type_id IN (
   279→        SELECT id FROM pm_types
   280→        WHERE pm_code = ANY(satisfied_codes)
   281→        AND fleet_id = (
   282→            SELECT fleet_id FROM trains WHERE id = NEW.train_id
   283→        )
   284→    );
   285→
   286→    -- Log the cascade
   287→    INSERT INTO audit_log (
   288→        entity_type, entity_id, action, user_id, details, created_at
   289→    ) VALUES (
   290→        'pm_schedule', NEW.id, 'PM_HIERARCHY_CASCADE',
   291→        NEW.completed_by,
   292→        jsonb_build_object(
   293→            'source_pm', pm_code,
   294→            'satisfied_pms', satisfied_codes,
   295→            'train_id', NEW.train_id
   296→        ),
   297→        NOW()
   298→    );
   299→
   300→    RETURN NEW;
   301→END;
   302→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
   303→
   304→-- Trigger for PM schedule completion
   305→DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS trg_pm_hierarchy_satisfaction ON pm_schedule;
   306→CREATE TRIGGER trg_pm_hierarchy_satisfaction
   307→    AFTER UPDATE ON pm_schedule
   308→    FOR EACH ROW
   309→    EXECUTE FUNCTION apply_pm_hierarchy_satisfaction();
   310→
   311→-- =============================================================================
   312→-- AUDIT TRAIL TRIGGERS
   313→-- =============================================================================
   314→
   315→-- Automatic audit logging for pm_schedule changes
   316→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION audit_pm_schedule_changes()
   317→RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
   318→BEGIN
   319→    INSERT INTO pm_schedule_history (
   320→        pm_schedule_id,
   321→        change_type,
   322→        old_values,
   323→        new_values,
   324→        changed_by,
   325→        change_reason,
   326→        created_at
   327→    ) VALUES (
   328→        COALESCE(NEW.id, OLD.id),
   329→        TG_OP,
   330→        CASE WHEN TG_OP IN ('UPDATE', 'DELETE') THEN to_jsonb(OLD) ELSE NULL END,
   331→        CASE WHEN TG_OP IN ('INSERT', 'UPDATE') THEN to_jsonb(NEW) ELSE NULL END,
   332→        COALESCE(NEW.updated_by, OLD.updated_by, current_setting('app.current_user_id', TRUE)::UUID),
   333→        current_setting('app.change_reason', TRUE),
   334→        NOW()
   335→    );
   336→
   337→    RETURN COALESCE(NEW, OLD);
   338→END;
   339→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
   340→
   341→DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS trg_audit_pm_schedule ON pm_schedule;
   342→CREATE TRIGGER trg_audit_pm_schedule
   343→    AFTER INSERT OR UPDATE OR DELETE ON pm_schedule
   344→    FOR EACH ROW
   345→    EXECUTE FUNCTION audit_pm_schedule_changes();
   346→
   347→-- =============================================================================
   348→-- VERIFICATION
   349→-- =============================================================================
   350→
   351→DO $$
   352→DECLARE
   353→    func_count INTEGER;
   354→    trigger_count INTEGER;
   355→BEGIN
   356→    -- Verify functions exist
   357→    SELECT COUNT(*) INTO func_count
   358→    FROM pg_proc
   359→    WHERE proname IN (
   360→        'update_updated_at_column',
   361→        'sync_train_fitness_from_unavailability',
   362→        'refresh_canvas_views',
   363→        'cleanup_expired_scenarios',
   364→        'release_expired_reservations',
   365→        'update_fleet_mileage_targets',
   366→        'apply_pm_hierarchy_satisfaction',
   367→        'audit_pm_schedule_changes'
   368→    );
   369→
   370→    IF func_count < 8 THEN
   371→        RAISE WARNING 'Not all functions created. Expected 8, found %', func_count;
   372→    END IF;
   373→
   374→    -- Verify triggers exist
   375→    SELECT COUNT(*) INTO trigger_count
   376→    FROM pg_trigger
   377→    WHERE tgname IN (
   378→        'trg_sync_train_fitness',
   379→        'trg_pm_hierarchy_satisfaction',
   380→        'trg_audit_pm_schedule'
   381→    );
   382→
   383→    IF trigger_count < 3 THEN
   384→        RAISE WARNING 'Not all triggers created. Expected 3, found %', trigger_count;
   385→    END IF;
   386→
   387→    RAISE NOTICE 'Database functions and triggers verified: % functions, % triggers',
   388→        func_count, trigger_count;
   389→END $$;

     8→-- =============================================================================
     9→-- STRATEGIC CANVAS VIEW
    10→-- =============================================================================
    11→
    12→-- Yearly PM aggregates per train for strategic (10-year) view
    13→-- Optimized for zoom levels 0-8 in deck.gl
    14→CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW IF NOT EXISTS v_canvas_strategic AS
    15→WITH yearly_pms AS (
    16→    SELECT
    17→        ps.train_id,
    18→        t.train_number,
    19→        t.fleet_id,
    20→        f.code AS fleet_code,
    21→        DATE_TRUNC('year', ps.target_date)::DATE AS year_start,
    22→        COUNT(*) AS pm_count,
    23→        COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE pt.pm_code = 'C4') AS c4_count,
    24→        COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE pt.pm_code = 'C3') AS c3_count,
    25→        COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE pt.pm_code = 'C2') AS c2_count,
    26→        COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE pt.pm_code = 'C1') AS c1_count,
    27→        COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE pt.pm_code = 'OVERHAUL') AS overhaul_count,
    28→        SUM(pt.estimated_hours) AS total_hours,
    29→        SUM(CASE WHEN ps.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS completed_count,
    30→        SUM(CASE WHEN ps.status = 'DEFERRED' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS deferred_count,
    31→        -- Bow wave indicator: deferred PMs accumulating
    32→        SUM(CASE
    33→            WHEN ps.status IN ('SCHEDULED', 'DEFERRED')
    34→            AND ps.target_date < CURRENT_DATE
    35→            THEN 1 ELSE 0
    36→        END) AS overdue_count
    37→    FROM pm_schedule ps
    38→    JOIN trains t ON ps.train_id = t.id
    39→    JOIN fleets f ON t.fleet_id = f.id
    40→    JOIN pm_types pt ON ps.pm_type_id = pt.id
    41→    WHERE ps.target_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 year'
    42→    AND ps.target_date <= CURRENT_DATE + INTERVAL '10 years'
    43→    GROUP BY
    44→        ps.train_id,
    45→        t.train_number,
    46→        t.fleet_id,
    47→        f.code,
    48→        DATE_TRUNC('year', ps.target_date)
    49→)
    50→SELECT
    51→    train_id,
    52→    train_number,
    53→    fleet_id,
    54→    fleet_code,
    55→    year_start,
    56→    pm_count,
    57→    c4_count,
    58→    c3_count,
    59→    c2_count,
    60→    c1_count,
    61→    overhaul_count,
    62→    total_hours,
    63→    completed_count,
    64→    deferred_count,
    65→    overdue_count,
    66→    -- Heatmap intensity (0-1 scale)
    67→    LEAST(1.0, pm_count / 12.0)::NUMERIC(3,2) AS pm_intensity,
    68→    -- Risk score (0-1 scale based on overdue)
    69→    LEAST(1.0, overdue_count / 5.0)::NUMERIC(3,2) AS risk_score
    70→FROM yearly_pms
    71→ORDER BY fleet_code, train_number, year_start;
    72→
    73→-- Unique index required for CONCURRENT refresh
    74→CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_v_canvas_strategic_pk
    75→    ON v_canvas_strategic (train_id, year_start);
    76→
    77→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_v_canvas_strategic_fleet
    78→    ON v_canvas_strategic (fleet_id, year_start);
    79→
    80→-- =============================================================================
    81→-- TACTICAL CANVAS VIEW
    82→-- =============================================================================
    83→
    84→-- Weekly PM details for tactical (12-week) view
    85→-- Optimized for zoom levels 8-14 in Konva
    86→CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW IF NOT EXISTS v_canvas_tactical AS
    87→WITH weekly_pms AS (
    88→    SELECT
    89→        ps.id AS pm_schedule_id,
    90→        ps.train_id,
    91→        t.train_number,
    92→        t.fleet_id,
    93→        f.code AS fleet_code,
    94→        t.fitness_status,
    95→        ps.pm_type_id,
    96→        pt.pm_code,
    97→        pt.estimated_hours,
    98→        ps.target_date,
    99→        DATE_TRUNC('week', ps.target_date)::DATE AS week_start,
   100→        ps.status,
   101→        ps.is_locked,
   102→        ps.maximo_status,
   103→        ps.assigned_depot_id,
   104→        d.code AS depot_code,
   105→        ps.assigned_track_id,
   106→        tr.track_number,
   107→        ps.constraint_window_start,
   108→        ps.constraint_window_end,
   109→        -- Days until compliance window end
   110→        (ps.constraint_window_end - CURRENT_DATE) AS days_to_deadline,
   111→        -- Urgency classification
   112→        CASE
   113→            WHEN ps.constraint_window_end < CURRENT_DATE THEN 'OVERDUE'
   114→            WHEN ps.constraint_window_end < CURRENT_DATE + INTERVAL '7 days' THEN 'URGENT'
   115→            WHEN ps.constraint_window_end < CURRENT_DATE + INTERVAL '14 days' THEN 'WARNING'
   116→            ELSE 'NORMAL'
   117→        END AS urgency
   118→    FROM pm_schedule ps
   119→    JOIN trains t ON ps.train_id = t.id
   120→    JOIN fleets f ON t.fleet_id = f.id
   121→    JOIN pm_types pt ON ps.pm_type_id = pt.id
   122→    LEFT JOIN depots d ON ps.assigned_depot_id = d.id
   123→    LEFT JOIN tracks tr ON ps.assigned_track_id = tr.id
   124→    WHERE ps.target_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '4 weeks'
   125→    AND ps.target_date <= CURRENT_DATE + INTERVAL '12 weeks'
   126→    AND ps.status NOT IN ('CANCELLED')
   127→)
   128→SELECT
   129→    pm_schedule_id,
   130→    train_id,
   131→    train_number,
   132→    fleet_id,
   133→    fleet_code,
   134→    fitness_status,
   135→    pm_type_id,
   136→    pm_code,
   137→    estimated_hours,
   138→    target_date,
   139→    week_start,
   140→    status,
   141→    is_locked,
   142→    maximo_status,
   143→    assigned_depot_id,
   144→    depot_code,
   145→    assigned_track_id,
   146→    track_number,
   147→    constraint_window_start,
   148→    constraint_window_end,
   149→    days_to_deadline,
   150→    urgency,
   151→    -- Gantt block positioning (row = train, column = date)
   152→    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY train_id ORDER BY target_date) AS block_order
   153→FROM weekly_pms
   154→ORDER BY fleet_code, train_number, target_date;

   156→-- Unique index required for CONCURRENT refresh
   157→CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_v_canvas_tactical_pk
   158→    ON v_canvas_tactical (pm_schedule_id);
   159→
   160→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_v_canvas_tactical_train
   161→    ON v_canvas_tactical (train_id, target_date);
   162→

   169→-- =============================================================================
   170→-- MAN-HOUR CANVAS VIEW (Regular View)
   171→-- =============================================================================
   172→
   173→-- Man-hour utilization overlay for canvas
   174→-- Regular view (not materialized) for real-time accuracy
   175→CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW v_manhour_canvas AS
   176→WITH depot_weekly AS (
   177→    SELECT
   178→        mp.depot_id,
   179→        d.code AS depot_code,
   180→        mp.period_start,
   181→        mp.period_end,
   182→        mp.required_pm_hours,
   183→        mp.required_corrective_hours,
   184→        mp.required_overhaul_hours,
   185→        mp.required_total_hours,
   186→        mp.available_regular_hours,
   187→        mp.available_overtime_hours,
   188→        mp.available_total_hours,
   189→        -- Calculated metrics
   190→        (mp.required_total_hours - mp.available_total_hours) AS variance_hours,
   191→        CASE
   192→            WHEN mp.available_total_hours > 0
   193→            THEN (mp.required_total_hours / mp.available_total_hours * 100)::NUMERIC(5,1)
   194→            ELSE 0
   195→        END AS utilization_pct,
   196→        -- Status flags
   197→        mp.required_total_hours > mp.available_total_hours AS is_overloaded,
   198→        mp.required_total_hours < (mp.available_total_hours * 0.70) AS is_underutilized,
   199→        -- Color coding for visualization
   200→        CASE
   201→            WHEN mp.required_total_hours > mp.available_total_hours THEN 'RED'
   202→            WHEN mp.required_total_hours > (mp.available_total_hours * 0.95) THEN 'ORANGE'
   203→            WHEN mp.required_total_hours > (mp.available_total_hours * 0.85) THEN 'YELLOW'
   204→            WHEN mp.required_total_hours < (mp.available_total_hours * 0.70) THEN 'BLUE'
   205→            ELSE 'GREEN'
   206→        END AS status_color
   207→    FROM manhour_projections mp
   208→    JOIN depots d ON mp.depot_id = d.id
   209→    WHERE mp.period_start >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '4 weeks'
   210→    AND mp.period_end <= CURRENT_DATE + INTERVAL '12 weeks'
   211→)
   212→SELECT
   213→    depot_id,
   214→    depot_code,
   215→    period_start,
   216→    period_end,
   217→    required_pm_hours,
   218→    required_corrective_hours,
   219→    required_overhaul_hours,
   220→    required_total_hours,
   221→    available_regular_hours,
   222→    available_overtime_hours,
   223→    available_total_hours,
   224→    variance_hours,
   225→    utilization_pct,
   226→    is_overloaded,
   227→    is_underutilized,
   228→    status_color
   229→FROM depot_weekly
   230→ORDER BY depot_code, period_start;

   232→-- =============================================================================
   233→-- DRIFT CANVAS VIEW (Regular View)
   234→-- =============================================================================
   235→
   236→-- Drift severity overlay for baseline comparison
   237→-- Regular view (not materialized) for real-time accuracy
   238→CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW v_drift_canvas AS
   239→SELECT
   240→    da.train_id,
   241→    t.train_number,
   242→    t.fleet_id,
   243→    f.code AS fleet_code,
   244→    da.baseline_id,
   245→    pb.name AS baseline_name,
   246→    pb.version AS baseline_version,
   247→    da.analysis_date,
   248→    -- Mileage drift
   249→    da.baseline_projected_km,
   250→    da.actual_km,
   251→    (da.actual_km - da.baseline_projected_km) AS mileage_drift_km,
   252→    da.mileage_drift_pct,
   253→    -- Schedule drift
   254→    da.avg_pm_drift_days,
   255→    da.max_pm_drift_days,
   256→    -- Replan requirement
   257→    da.requires_replan,
   258→    da.replan_reason,
   259→    -- Severity classification
   260→    CASE
   261→        WHEN da.requires_replan THEN 'CRITICAL'
   262→        WHEN ABS(da.mileage_drift_pct) > 3.0 THEN 'HIGH'
   263→        WHEN ABS(da.mileage_drift_pct) > 2.0 THEN 'MEDIUM'
   264→        WHEN ABS(da.mileage_drift_pct) > 1.0 THEN 'LOW'
   265→        ELSE 'NONE'
   266→    END AS drift_severity,
   267→    -- Visual overlay data

-- MMS Materialized Views Migration
-- Pre-computed views for canvas rendering performance
--
-- Run order: 4 (after schema and functions)
-- @see specs/003-database-schema.md Section 3.11
-- @see specs/006-ui-specification.md Section 6.2

-- =============================================================================
-- STRATEGIC CANVAS VIEW
-- =============================================================================

-- Yearly PM aggregates per train for strategic (10-year) view
-- Optimized for zoom levels 0-8 in deck.gl
CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW IF NOT EXISTS v_canvas_strategic AS
WITH yearly_pms AS (
    SELECT
        ps.train_id,
        t.train_number,
        t.fleet_id,
        f.code AS fleet_code,
        DATE_TRUNC('year', ps.target_date)::DATE AS year_start,
        COUNT(*) AS pm_count,
        COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE pt.pm_code = 'C4') AS c4_count,
        COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE pt.pm_code = 'C3') AS c3_count,
        COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE pt.pm_code = 'C2') AS c2_count,
        COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE pt.pm_code = 'C1') AS c1_count,
        COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE pt.pm_code = 'OVERHAUL') AS overhaul_count,
        SUM(pt.estimated_hours) AS total_hours,
        SUM(CASE WHEN ps.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS completed_count,
        SUM(CASE WHEN ps.status = 'DEFERRED' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS deferred_count,
        -- Bow wave indicator: deferred PMs accumulating
        SUM(CASE
            WHEN ps.status IN ('SCHEDULED', 'DEFERRED')
            AND ps.target_date < CURRENT_DATE
            THEN 1 ELSE 0
        END) AS overdue_count
    FROM pm_schedule ps
    JOIN trains t ON ps.train_id = t.id
    JOIN fleets f ON t.fleet_id = f.id
    JOIN pm_types pt ON ps.pm_type_id = pt.id
    WHERE ps.target_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 year'
    AND ps.target_date <= CURRENT_DATE + INTERVAL '10 years'
    GROUP BY
        ps.train_id,
        t.train_number,
        t.fleet_id,
        f.code,
        DATE_TRUNC('year', ps.target_date)
)
SELECT
    train_id,
    train_number,
    fleet_id,
    fleet_code,
    year_start,
    pm_count,
    c4_count,
    c3_count,
    c2_count,
    c1_count,
    overhaul_count,
    total_hours,
    completed_count,
    deferred_count,
    overdue_count,
    -- Heatmap intensity (0-1 scale)
    LEAST(1.0, pm_count / 12.0)::NUMERIC(3,2) AS pm_intensity,
    -- Risk score (0-1 scale based on overdue)
    LEAST(1.0, overdue_count / 5.0)::NUMERIC(3,2) AS risk_score
FROM yearly_pms
ORDER BY fleet_code, train_number, year_start;

-- Unique index required for CONCURRENT refresh
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_v_canvas_strategic_pk
    ON v_canvas_strategic (train_id, year_start);

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_v_canvas_strategic_fleet
    ON v_canvas_strategic (fleet_id, year_start);

-- =============================================================================
-- TACTICAL CANVAS VIEW
-- =============================================================================

-- Weekly PM details for tactical (12-week) view
-- Optimized for zoom levels 8-14 in Konva
CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW IF NOT EXISTS v_canvas_tactical AS
WITH weekly_pms AS (
    SELECT
        ps.id AS pm_schedule_id,
        ps.train_id,
        t.train_number,
        t.fleet_id,
        f.code AS fleet_code,
        t.fitness_status,
        ps.pm_type_id,
        pt.pm_code,
        pt.estimated_hours,
        ps.target_date,
        DATE_TRUNC('week', ps.target_date)::DATE AS week_start,
        ps.status,
        ps.is_locked,
        ps.maximo_status,
        ps.assigned_depot_id,
        d.code AS depot_code,
        ps.assigned_track_id,
        tr.track_number,
        ps.constraint_window_start,
        ps.constraint_window_end,
        -- Days until compliance window end
        (ps.constraint_window_end - CURRENT_DATE) AS days_to_deadline,
        -- Urgency classification
        CASE
            WHEN ps.constraint_window_end < CURRENT_DATE THEN 'OVERDUE'
            WHEN ps.constraint_window_end < CURRENT_DATE + INTERVAL '7 days' THEN 'URGENT'
            WHEN ps.constraint_window_end < CURRENT_DATE + INTERVAL '14 days' THEN 'WARNING'
            ELSE 'NORMAL'
        END AS urgency
    FROM pm_schedule ps
    JOIN trains t ON ps.train_id = t.id
    JOIN fleets f ON t.fleet_id = f.id
    JOIN pm_types pt ON ps.pm_type_id = pt.id
    LEFT JOIN depots d ON ps.assigned_depot_id = d.id
    LEFT JOIN tracks tr ON ps.assigned_track_id = tr.id
    WHERE ps.target_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '4 weeks'
    AND ps.target_date <= CURRENT_DATE + INTERVAL '12 weeks'
    AND ps.status NOT IN ('CANCELLED')
)
SELECT
    pm_schedule_id,
    train_id,
    train_number,
    fleet_id,
    fleet_code,
    fitness_status,
    pm_type_id,
    pm_code,
    estimated_hours,
    target_date,
    week_start,
    status,
    is_locked,
    maximo_status,
    assigned_depot_id,
    depot_code,
    assigned_track_id,
    track_number,
    constraint_window_start,
    constraint_window_end,
    days_to_deadline,
    urgency,
    -- Gantt block positioning (row = train, column = date)
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY train_id ORDER BY target_date) AS block_order
FROM weekly_pms
ORDER BY fleet_code, train_number, target_date;

-- Unique index required for CONCURRENT refresh
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_v_canvas_tactical_pk
    ON v_canvas_tactical (pm_schedule_id);

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_v_canvas_tactical_train
    ON v_canvas_tactical (train_id, target_date);

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_v_canvas_tactical_week
    ON v_canvas_tactical (week_start, fleet_id);

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_v_canvas_tactical_urgency
    ON v_canvas_tactical (urgency, target_date);

-- =============================================================================
-- MAN-HOUR CANVAS VIEW (Regular View)
-- =============================================================================

-- Man-hour utilization overlay for canvas
-- Regular view (not materialized) for real-time accuracy
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW v_manhour_canvas AS
WITH depot_weekly AS (
    SELECT
        mp.depot_id,
        d.code AS depot_code,
        mp.period_start,
        mp.period_end,
        mp.required_pm_hours,
        mp.required_corrective_hours,
        mp.required_overhaul_hours,
        mp.required_total_hours,
        mp.available_regular_hours,
        mp.available_overtime_hours,
        mp.available_total_hours,
        -- Calculated metrics
        (mp.required_total_hours - mp.available_total_hours) AS variance_hours,
        CASE
            WHEN mp.available_total_hours > 0
            THEN (mp.required_total_hours / mp.available_total_hours * 100)::NUMERIC(5,1)
            ELSE 0
        END AS utilization_pct,
        -- Status flags
        mp.required_total_hours > mp.available_total_hours AS is_overloaded,
        mp.required_total_hours < (mp.available_total_hours * 0.70) AS is_underutilized,
        -- Color coding for visualization
        CASE
            WHEN mp.required_total_hours > mp.available_total_hours THEN 'RED'
            WHEN mp.required_total_hours > (mp.available_total_hours * 0.95) THEN 'ORANGE'
            WHEN mp.required_total_hours > (mp.available_total_hours * 0.85) THEN 'YELLOW'
            WHEN mp.required_total_hours < (mp.available_total_hours * 0.70) THEN 'BLUE'
            ELSE 'GREEN'
        END AS status_color
    FROM manhour_projections mp
    JOIN depots d ON mp.depot_id = d.id
    WHERE mp.period_start >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '4 weeks'
    AND mp.period_end <= CURRENT_DATE + INTERVAL '12 weeks'
)
SELECT
    depot_id,
    depot_code,
    period_start,
    period_end,
    required_pm_hours,
    required_corrective_hours,
    required_overhaul_hours,
    required_total_hours,
    available_regular_hours,
    available_overtime_hours,
    available_total_hours,
    variance_hours,
    utilization_pct,
    is_overloaded,
    is_underutilized,
    status_color
FROM depot_weekly
ORDER BY depot_code, period_start;

-- =============================================================================
-- DRIFT CANVAS VIEW (Regular View)
-- =============================================================================

-- Drift severity overlay for baseline comparison
-- Regular view (not materialized) for real-time accuracy
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW v_drift_canvas AS
SELECT
    da.train_id,
    t.train_number,
    t.fleet_id,
    f.code AS fleet_code,
    da.baseline_id,
    pb.name AS baseline_name,
    pb.version AS baseline_version,
    da.analysis_date,
    -- Mileage drift
    da.baseline_projected_km,
    da.actual_km,
    (da.actual_km - da.baseline_projected_km) AS mileage_drift_km,
    da.mileage_drift_pct,
    -- Schedule drift
    da.avg_pm_drift_days,
    da.max_pm_drift_days,
    -- Replan requirement
    da.requires_replan,
    da.replan_reason,
    -- Severity classification
    CASE
        WHEN da.requires_replan THEN 'CRITICAL'
        WHEN ABS(da.mileage_drift_pct) > 3.0 THEN 'HIGH'
        WHEN ABS(da.mileage_drift_pct) > 2.0 THEN 'MEDIUM'
        WHEN ABS(da.mileage_drift_pct) > 1.0 THEN 'LOW'
        ELSE 'NONE'
    END AS drift_severity,
    -- Visual overlay data
    CASE
        WHEN da.requires_replan THEN 'RED'
        WHEN ABS(da.mileage_drift_pct) > 3.0 THEN 'ORANGE'
        WHEN ABS(da.mileage_drift_pct) > 2.0 THEN 'YELLOW'
        WHEN ABS(da.mileage_drift_pct) > 1.0 THEN 'LIGHT_YELLOW'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS overlay_color,
    -- Shadow offset for baseline comparison (px)
    CASE
        WHEN da.avg_pm_drift_days IS NOT NULL
        THEN ROUND(da.avg_pm_drift_days * 2)::INTEGER  -- 2px per day drift
        ELSE 0
    END AS shadow_offset_days
FROM drift_analysis da
JOIN trains t ON da.train_id = t.id
JOIN fleets f ON t.fleet_id = f.id
JOIN plan_baselines pb ON da.baseline_id = pb.id
WHERE da.analysis_date = (
    -- Latest analysis only
    SELECT MAX(analysis_date)
    FROM drift_analysis
    WHERE baseline_id = da.baseline_id
)
AND pb.status = 'PUBLISHED'
ORDER BY f.code, t.train_number;

-- =============================================================================
-- INITIAL REFRESH
-- =============================================================================

-- Populate materialized views with initial data
-- Note: This will fail if tables are empty (OK for fresh install)
DO $$
BEGIN
    -- Check if pm_schedule has data before refreshing
    IF EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM pm_schedule LIMIT 1) THEN
        REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW v_canvas_strategic;
        REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW v_canvas_tactical;
        RAISE NOTICE 'Canvas materialized views refreshed';
    ELSE
        RAISE NOTICE 'pm_schedule is empty - skipping initial refresh';
    END IF;
EXCEPTION
    WHEN OTHERS THEN
        RAISE NOTICE 'Initial refresh skipped: %', SQLERRM;
END $$;

-- =============================================================================
-- VERIFICATION
-- =============================================================================

DO $$
DECLARE
    view_count INTEGER;
BEGIN
    SELECT COUNT(*) INTO view_count
    FROM pg_catalog.pg_class c
    JOIN pg_catalog.pg_namespace n ON n.oid = c.relnamespace
    WHERE n.nspname = 'public'
    AND c.relname IN (
        'v_canvas_strategic',
        'v_canvas_tactical',
        'v_manhour_canvas',
        'v_drift_canvas'
    );

    IF view_count < 4 THEN
        RAISE WARNING 'Not all views created. Expected 4, found %', view_count;
    ELSE
        RAISE NOTICE 'All canvas views created successfully';
    END IF;
END $$;

1→# Metro Fleet Mileage Management System
     2→# Part 3: Database Schema
     3→
     4→**Document Version:** 2.1  
     5→**Status:** APPROVED FOR DEVELOPMENT  
     6→**Last Updated:** 2026-01-19
     7→
     8→---
     9→
    10→## 3.1 Schema Overview
    11→
    12→The database schema is organized into the following domains:
    13→
    14→| Domain | Tables | Purpose |
    15→|--------|--------|---------|
    16→| Core Fleet | `fleets`, `trains`, `depots`, `routes` | Asset master data |
    17→| **Operational Constraints** | `service_demand`, `route_utilization_profiles`, `train_route_compatibility` | **Operational requirements and constraints** |
    18→| **Unavailability** | `unavailability_events` | **Corrective maintenance and incident tracking** |
    19→| Mileage Tracking | `mileage_readings`, `meter_events` | Time-series odometer data |
    20→| Component Tracking | `train_components`, `component_mileage` | Component-level lifecycle |
    21→| PM Management | `pm_types`, `pm_schedule`, `pm_material_requirements` | Maintenance planning |
    22→| **Track Capabilities** | `track_capabilities`, `pm_capability_requirements` | **Qualified resource matching** |
    23→| **Data Migration** | `pm_baseline_assumptions` | **Cold start and history inference** |
    24→| Material Management | `inventory_positions`, `material_reservations` | Parts availability |
    25→| Man-hour Planning | `manhour_capacity`, `manhour_projections`, `manhour_adjustments` | Workforce balancing |
    26→| Baseline & Drift | `plan_baselines`, `baseline_pm_schedule`, `baseline_mileage_projection` | Plan stability |
    27→| Scenario Management | `scenarios`, `scenario_deltas` | What-if analysis |
    28→| Costing | `labor_rates`, `material_costs`, `cost_baselines` | Financial tracking |
    29→| Audit & History | `pm_schedule_history`, `audit_log` | Change tracking |
    30→| Users & Auth | `users`, `roles`, `sessions` | Access control |
    31→
    32→> **v2.1 Changes:** Added operational constraints domain (service demand, route utilization, train-route compatibility), unavailability events, track capabilities with PM requirements, and data migration assumptions tables.
    33→
    34→---
    35→
    36→## 3.2 Core Fleet Tables
    37→
    38→```sql
    39→-- Enable required extensions
    40→CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS "uuid-ossp";
    41→CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS timescaledb;
    42→
    43→-- Enumerated types
    44→CREATE TYPE fitness_status AS ENUM ('FIT', 'RESTRICTED', 'GROUNDED');
    45→CREATE TYPE pm_status AS ENUM ('SCHEDULED', 'IN_PROGRESS', 'COMPLETED', 'CANCELLED', 'DEFERRED');
    46→CREATE TYPE track_type AS ENUM ('LIGHT', 'HEAVY', 'OVERHAUL', 'STABLING');
    47→CREATE TYPE shift_type AS ENUM ('DAY', 'NIGHT', 'WEEKEND', 'HOLIDAY');
    48→CREATE TYPE adjustment_status AS ENUM ('PROPOSED', 'APPROVED', 'REJECTED', 'APPLIED');
    49→CREATE TYPE baseline_status AS ENUM ('DRAFT', 'PUBLISHED', 'SUPERSEDED', 'ARCHIVED');
    50→CREATE TYPE scenario_status AS ENUM ('ACTIVE', 'PROMOTED', 'DISCARDED', 'EXPIRED');
    51→CREATE TYPE material_criticality AS ENUM ('BLOCKING', 'DEFERRABLE', 'CONSUMABLE');
    52→CREATE TYPE reservation_type AS ENUM ('SOFT', 'HARD');
    53→
    54→-- NEW: v2.1 enum types
    55→CREATE TYPE day_type AS ENUM ('WEEKDAY', 'SATURDAY', 'SUNDAY', 'HOLIDAY');
    56→CREATE TYPE unavailability_type AS ENUM ('CORRECTIVE', 'INCIDENT', 'COLLISION', 'VANDALISM', 'EXTERNAL', 'PLANNED_OUTAGE');
    57→CREATE TYPE unavailability_status AS ENUM ('ACTIVE', 'RESOLVED');
    58→CREATE TYPE track_capability AS ENUM ('PIT', 'CRANE', 'LIFTING_JACKS', 'WHEEL_LATHE', 'CATENARY_ISOLATION', 'ROOF_ACCESS', 'WASH', 'DROP_TABLE');
    59→CREATE TYPE constraint_type AS ENUM ('SIGNALING', 'GAUGE', 'POWER_SUPPLY', 'PLATFORM_LENGTH', 'MAX_SPEED', 'TUNNEL_CLEARANCE');
    60→CREATE TYPE assumption_type AS ENUM ('INFERRED_FROM_COMMISSIONING', 'INFERRED_FROM_HIGHER_PM', 'MANUAL_OVERRIDE', 'IMPORTED', 'NO_HISTORY');
    61→CREATE TYPE confidence_level AS ENUM ('HIGH', 'MEDIUM', 'LOW', 'ESTIMATED');
    62→
    63→-- Depots
    64→CREATE TABLE depots (
    65→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    66→    code VARCHAR(10) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    67→    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    68→    location VARCHAR(200),
    69→    timezone VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT 'Asia/Singapore',
    70→    active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
    71→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    72→    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
    73→);
    74→
    75→-- Fleets (train types/manufacturers)
    76→CREATE TABLE fleets (
    77→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    78→    code VARCHAR(10) NOT NULL UNIQUE,  -- 'KS', 'ALSTOM'
    79→    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    80→    manufacturer VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    81→    model VARCHAR(100),
    82→    base_depot_id UUID REFERENCES depots(id),
    83→    cars_per_train INTEGER DEFAULT 6,
    84→    active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
    85→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    86→    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
    87→);
    88→
    89→-- Trains
    90→CREATE TABLE trains (
    91→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    92→    fleet_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES fleets(id),
    93→    train_number VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    94→    commissioning_date DATE,
    95→    current_mileage_km INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    96→    fitness_status fitness_status DEFAULT 'FIT',
    97→    fitness_notes TEXT,
    98→    current_depot_id UUID REFERENCES depots(id),
    99→    home_depot_id UUID REFERENCES depots(id),
   100→    active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
   101→    
   102→    -- For mileage balancing
   103→    target_mileage_km INTEGER,  -- Calculated target for fleet balance
   104→    mileage_variance_km INTEGER GENERATED ALWAYS AS 
   105→        (current_mileage_km - COALESCE(target_mileage_km, current_mileage_km)) STORED,
   106→    
   107→    -- Canvas display properties
   108→    display_color VARCHAR(7),
   109→    display_order INTEGER,
   110→    
   111→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   112→    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
   113→);
   114→
   115→CREATE INDEX idx_trains_fleet ON trains(fleet_id);
   116→CREATE INDEX idx_trains_fitness ON trains(fitness_status) WHERE active = TRUE;
   117→CREATE INDEX idx_trains_depot ON trains(current_depot_id);
   118→
   119→-- Routes
   120→CREATE TABLE routes (
   121→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   122→    route_code VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
   123→    route_name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
   124→    line_id VARCHAR(20),
   125→    expected_km_per_day INTEGER NOT NULL,
   126→    required_trains INTEGER NOT NULL,
   127→    
   128→    -- NEW v2.1: Route characteristics for compatibility matching
   129→    default_daily_km INTEGER,                        -- Default mileage for projection
   130→    signaling_system VARCHAR(30),                    -- 'ATC', 'CBTC', 'ERTMS', 'LEGACY'
   131→    power_system VARCHAR(30),                        -- 'THIRD_RAIL_750V', 'OVERHEAD_25KV', 'OVERHEAD_1500V'
   132→    tunnel_clearance_mm INTEGER,                     -- Maximum vehicle width allowed
   133→    platform_length_m DECIMAL(5,2),                  -- Platform length constraint
   134→    max_axle_load_tonnes DECIMAL(4,2),               -- Track weight limit
   135→    
   136→    -- Restrictions
   137→    allowed_fleet_ids UUID[],
   138→    requires_ato BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
   139→    min_cars INTEGER,
   140→    max_cars INTEGER,
   141→    
   142→    active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
   143→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   144→    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
   145→);
   146→
   147→-- Tracks (maintenance bays)
   148→CREATE TABLE tracks (
   149→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   150→    depot_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES depots(id),
   151→    track_number VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,
   152→    track_type track_type NOT NULL,
   153→    max_train_length_m DECIMAL(5,2),
   154→    has_overhead_crane BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
   155→    has_pit_access BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
   156→    
   157→    active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
   158→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   159→    
   160→    UNIQUE(depot_id, track_number)
   161→);
   162→
   163→CREATE INDEX idx_tracks_depot_type ON tracks(depot_id, track_type) WHERE active = TRUE;
   164→```
   165→
   166→---
   167→
   168→## 3.2.1 Service Demand Tables (NEW v2.1)
   169→
   170→Service demand defines the minimum number of trains required for passenger operations by day type. This is a **hard constraint** for the optimizer - it cannot schedule maintenance that would violate availability targets.
   171→
   172→```sql
   173→-- Service demand by fleet and day type
   174→CREATE TABLE service_demand (
   175→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   176→    fleet_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES fleets(id),
   177→    effective_date DATE NOT NULL,

   260→## 3.2.3 Unavailability Events (NEW v2.1)
   261→
   262→Unavailability events track trains that cannot be scheduled due to corrective maintenance, incidents, or other blocking factors. This is a **critical integration point** with Maximo CMMS.
   263→
   264→```sql
   265→-- Unavailability events (corrective maintenance, incidents, etc.)
   266→CREATE TABLE unavailability_events (
   267→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   268→    train_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES trains(id),
   269→    
   270→    -- Event classification
   271→    event_type unavailability_type NOT NULL,  -- 'CORRECTIVE', 'INCIDENT', etc.
   272→    status unavailability_status NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ACTIVE',
   273→    restriction_level fitness_status NOT NULL, -- Uses existing ENUM: 'RESTRICTED' or 'GROUNDED'
   274→    
   275→    -- Timing
   276→    reported_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
   277→    estimated_repair_hours INTEGER,
   278→    estimated_return_date DATE,
   279→    actual_return_date DATE,
   280→    
   281→    -- External system linkage
   282→    source_system VARCHAR(30),        -- 'MAXIMO', 'OCC', 'MANUAL'
   283→    external_reference VARCHAR(50),   -- Maximo WO number (e.g., 'WO-2026-001234')
   284→    external_status VARCHAR(30),      -- Maximo status (e.g., 'INPRG', 'WAPPR')
   285→    
   286→    -- Description
   287→    description TEXT,
   288→    failure_code VARCHAR(30),         -- Standard failure classification
   289→    affected_system VARCHAR(50),      -- 'TRACTION', 'BRAKES', 'DOORS', 'HVAC', etc.
   290→    
   291→    -- Scheduling impact flags
   292→    blocks_pm_scheduling BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
   293→    blocks_run_assignment BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
   294→    
   295→    -- Resolution
   296→    resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   297→    resolved_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
   298→    resolution_notes TEXT,
   299→    
   300→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   301→    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   302→    
   303→    -- Constraint: Cannot have resolved data if status is ACTIVE
   304→    CONSTRAINT chk_resolved_status CHECK (
   305→        (status = 'ACTIVE' AND resolved_at IS NULL) OR
   306→        (status = 'RESOLVED' AND resolved_at IS NOT NULL)
   307→    )
   308→);
   309→
   310→-- Primary query: Find active unavailability for a train
   311→CREATE INDEX idx_unavailability_active ON unavailability_events(train_id, status) 
   312→    WHERE status = 'ACTIVE';
   313→-- Dashboard query: All active unavailability by estimated return
   314→CREATE INDEX idx_unavailability_return ON unavailability_events(estimated_return_date) 
   315→    WHERE status = 'ACTIVE';
   316→-- Maximo sync: Find by external reference
   317→CREATE INDEX idx_unavailability_external ON unavailability_events(source_system, external_reference);
   318→-- Audit: Find by type and date range
   319→CREATE INDEX idx_unavailability_audit ON unavailability_events(event_type, reported_at DESC);
   320→
   321→-- Trigger to update train fitness_status when unavailability changes
   322→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION sync_train_fitness_from_unavailability()
   323→RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
   324→BEGIN
   325→    IF TG_OP = 'INSERT' OR TG_OP = 'UPDATE' THEN
   326→        IF NEW.status = 'ACTIVE' THEN
   327→            UPDATE trains 
   328→            SET fitness_status = NEW.restriction_level,
   329→                fitness_notes = COALESCE(NEW.description, 'Unavailability event: ' || NEW.event_type),
   330→                updated_at = NOW()
   331→            WHERE id = NEW.train_id;
   332→        ELSIF NEW.status = 'RESOLVED' THEN
   333→            -- Only reset to FIT if no other active unavailability exists
   334→            IF NOT EXISTS (
   335→                SELECT 1 FROM unavailability_events 
   336→                WHERE train_id = NEW.train_id 
   337→                AND status = 'ACTIVE' 
   338→                AND id != NEW.id
   339→            ) THEN
   340→                UPDATE trains 
   341→                SET fitness_status = 'FIT',
   342→                    fitness_notes = NULL,
   343→                    updated_at = NOW()
   344→                WHERE id = NEW.train_id;
   345→            END IF;
   346→        END IF;
   347→    END IF;
   348→    RETURN NEW;
   349→END;
   350→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
   351→
   352→CREATE TRIGGER trg_sync_train_fitness
   353→AFTER INSERT OR UPDATE ON unavailability_events
   354→FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION sync_train_fitness_from_unavailability();
   355→```

   359→## 3.2.4 Train-Route Compatibility (NEW v2.1)
   360→
   361→Defines which trains can operate on which routes based on technical constraints (signaling, gauge, power supply, etc.). Prevents the optimizer from assigning trains to incompatible routes.
   362→
   363→```sql
   364→-- Train-route compatibility matrix
   365→CREATE TABLE train_route_compatibility (
   366→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   367→    
   368→    -- Can be at train or fleet level
   369→    train_id UUID REFERENCES trains(id),
   370→    fleet_id UUID REFERENCES fleets(id),
   371→    route_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES routes(id),
   372→    
   373→    -- Compatibility determination
   374→    is_compatible BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE,
   375→    constraint_type constraint_type,   -- 'SIGNALING', 'GAUGE', 'POWER_SUPPLY', etc.
   376→    restriction_reason TEXT,           -- Human-readable explanation
   377→    
   378→    -- Validity period (for temporary restrictions)
   379→    effective_date DATE DEFAULT CURRENT_DATE,
   380→    expiry_date DATE,                  -- NULL = permanent
   381→    
   382→    -- Metadata
   383→    verified_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
   384→    verified_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   385→    notes TEXT,
   386→    
   387→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   388→    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   389→    
   390→    -- Must specify either train_id OR fleet_id, not both
   391→    CONSTRAINT chk_train_or_fleet CHECK (
   392→        (train_id IS NOT NULL AND fleet_id IS NULL) OR
   393→        (train_id IS NULL AND fleet_id IS NOT NULL)
   394→    )
   395→);
   396→
   397→CREATE INDEX idx_compatibility_route ON train_route_compatibility(route_id, is_compatible);
   398→CREATE INDEX idx_compatibility_train ON train_route_compatibility(train_id, is_compatible) 
   399→    WHERE train_id IS NOT NULL;
   400→CREATE INDEX idx_compatibility_fleet ON train_route_compatibility(fleet_id, is_compatible) 
   401→    WHERE fleet_id IS NOT NULL;
   402→CREATE INDEX idx_compatibility_active ON train_route_compatibility(effective_date, expiry_date)
   403→    WHERE is_compatible = FALSE;

   478→## 3.2.6 PM Baseline Assumptions (NEW v2.1)
   479→
   480→Handles cold start scenarios where PM history is incomplete during data migration. Tracks assumptions with confidence levels and flags records requiring manual review.
   481→
   482→```sql
   483→-- PM history baseline assumptions for cold start scenarios
   484→CREATE TABLE pm_baseline_assumptions (
   485→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   486→    train_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES trains(id),
   487→    pm_type_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES pm_types(id),
   488→    
   489→    -- Assumption details
   490→    assumption_type assumption_type NOT NULL,
   491→    -- 'INFERRED_FROM_COMMISSIONING' - assume first PM at commissioning date + interval
   492→    -- 'INFERRED_FROM_HIGHER_PM' - derived from C4/OH completion implies C1/C2/C3 done
   493→    -- 'MANUAL_OVERRIDE' - operator entered value
   494→    -- 'IMPORTED' - from legacy system with known quality
   495→    -- 'NO_HISTORY' - no data available, using conservative estimate
   496→    
   497→    -- Assumed baseline values
   498→    assumed_last_pm_date DATE,
   499→    assumed_last_pm_mileage INTEGER,
   500→    confidence_level confidence_level NOT NULL,  -- 'HIGH', 'MEDIUM', 'LOW', 'ESTIMATED'
   501→    
   502→    -- Inference source documentation
   503→    inference_source TEXT,             -- Explanation of how assumption was derived
   504→    inference_data JSONB,              -- Supporting data (e.g., higher PM dates)
   505→    
   506→    -- Review workflow
   507→    requires_review BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
   508→    reviewed_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
   509→    reviewed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   510→    review_notes TEXT,
   511→    
   512→    -- When applied
   513→    applied_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   514→    applied_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
   515→    
   516→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   517→    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   518→    
   519→    UNIQUE(train_id, pm_type_id)
   520→);
   521→
   522→CREATE INDEX idx_assumptions_train ON pm_baseline_assumptions(train_id);
   523→CREATE INDEX idx_assumptions_review ON pm_baseline_assumptions(requires_review) 
   524→    WHERE requires_review = TRUE;
   525→CREATE INDEX idx_assumptions_confidence ON pm_baseline_assumptions(confidence_level)
   526→    WHERE confidence_level IN ('LOW', 'ESTIMATED');
   527→
   528→-- Example inference rules (implemented in application logic):
   529→-- 1. If train commissioned 2020-01-01 and C1 interval is 6 months:
   530→--    assumed_last_pm_date = 2020-01-01, confidence = 'LOW'
   531→-- 2. If C4 completed 2024-06-15 and C4 satisfies C1/C2/C3:
   532→--    For C1/C2/C3: assumed_last_pm_date = 2024-06-15, confidence = 'HIGH'
   533→-- 3. If no data at all:
   534→--    assumed_last_pm_date = commissioning_date, confidence = 'ESTIMATED', requires_review = TRUE
   535→```

   563→CREATE INDEX idx_meter_events_train ON meter_events(train_id, event_date DESC);
   564→
   565→-- Daily mileage readings (TimescaleDB hypertable)
   566→CREATE TABLE mileage_readings (
   567→    train_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES trains(id),
   568→    reading_date DATE NOT NULL,
   569→    
   570→    -- Raw and adjusted values
   571→    raw_reading INTEGER NOT NULL,        -- Odometer value from CSV
   572→    adjusted_reading INTEGER NOT NULL,   -- After meter event adjustments
   573→    daily_km INTEGER,                    -- Calculated: today - yesterday
   574→    
   575→    -- Validation
   576→    validation_status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'PENDING',  -- 'PENDING', 'VALID', 'ANOMALY', 'CORRECTED'
   577→    anomaly_type VARCHAR(30),            -- 'NEGATIVE', 'SPIKE', 'MISSING', 'DUPLICATE'
   578→    anomaly_notes TEXT,
   579→    
   580→    -- Source tracking
   581→    source_file VARCHAR(255),
   582→    meter_event_id UUID REFERENCES meter_events(id),
   583→    
   584→    ingested_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   585→    
   586→    PRIMARY KEY (train_id, reading_date)
   587→);
   588→
   589→-- Convert to TimescaleDB hypertable
   590→SELECT create_hypertable('mileage_readings', 'reading_date', 
   591→    chunk_time_interval => INTERVAL '7 days',
   592→    if_not_exists => TRUE
   593→);
   594→
   595→-- Compression policy (compress data older than 30 days)
   596→SELECT add_compression_policy('mileage_readings', INTERVAL '30 days');
   597→
   598→-- Retention policy (keep 13 years for contract + buffer)
   599→SELECT add_retention_policy('mileage_readings', INTERVAL '13 years');
   600→
   601→-- Indexes for common queries
   602→CREATE INDEX idx_mileage_train_date ON mileage_readings (train_id, reading_date DESC);
   603→CREATE INDEX idx_mileage_validation ON mileage_readings (validation_status, reading_date DESC) 
   604→    WHERE validation_status != 'VALID';
   605→
   606→-- Continuous aggregate for dashboard queries
   607→CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW daily_fleet_mileage
   608→WITH (timescaledb.continuous) AS
   609→SELECT 
   610→    time_bucket('1 day', reading_date) AS bucket,
   611→    t.fleet_id,
   612→    COUNT(*) as train_count,
   613→    SUM(daily_km) as total_daily_km,
   614→    AVG(daily_km) as avg_daily_km,
   615→    MIN(daily_km) as min_daily_km,
   616→    MAX(daily_km) as max_daily_km,
   617→    STDDEV(daily_km) as stddev_daily_km
   618→FROM mileage_readings mr
   619→JOIN trains t ON mr.train_id = t.id
   620→WHERE mr.validation_status = 'VALID'
   621→GROUP BY bucket, t.fleet_id
   622→WITH NO DATA;
   623→
   624→SELECT add_continuous_aggregate_policy('daily_fleet_mileage',
   625→    start_offset => INTERVAL '3 days',
   626→    end_offset => INTERVAL '1 hour',
   627→    schedule_interval => INTERVAL '1 hour'
   628→);
   629→```

   711→## 3.5 PM Management Tables
   712→
   713→```sql
   714→-- PM types (maintenance task definitions)
   715→CREATE TABLE pm_types (
   716→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   717→    fleet_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES fleets(id),
   718→    pm_code VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,      -- 'C1', 'C2', 'C3', 'C4', 'OVERHAUL'
   719→    pm_name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
   720→    version INTEGER DEFAULT 1,
   721→    
   722→    -- Interval thresholds (whichever comes first)
   723→    interval_km INTEGER,               -- Mileage-based trigger
   724→    interval_months INTEGER,           -- Time-based trigger (industry standard)
   725→    
   726→    -- PM Hierarchy: which lower-level PMs does this PM satisfy?
   727→    -- When a C4 is completed, it satisfies C1/C2/C3 requirements
   728→    -- This resets their mileage counters from the C4 completion point
   729→    -- Example: C4 row has satisfies_codes = ['C1', 'C2', 'C3']
   730→    satisfies_codes VARCHAR(20)[],
   731→    
   732→    -- Resource requirements
   733→    estimated_hours DECIMAL(6,2) NOT NULL,
   734→    track_type track_type NOT NULL,
   735→    min_track_days INTEGER DEFAULT 1,  -- How many days track is occupied
   736→    
   737→    -- Compliance window (tolerance for scheduling)
   738→    early_tolerance_pct DECIMAL(5,2) DEFAULT 10.0,   -- Can do 10% early
   739→    late_tolerance_pct DECIMAL(5,2) DEFAULT 10.0,    -- Can do 10% late
   740→    
   741→    -- Certification requirements
   742→    required_certifications VARCHAR(50)[],
   743→    
   744→    -- NEW v2.1: PM type versioning for interval changes over contract life
   745→    effective_from DATE NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_DATE,
   746→    effective_to DATE,                              -- NULL = current version
   747→    superseded_by UUID REFERENCES pm_types(id),    -- Link to newer version
   748→    
   749→    -- Canvas display
   750→    display_color VARCHAR(7),
   751→    display_priority INTEGER DEFAULT 50,
   752→    
   753→    active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
   754→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   755→    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   756→    
   757→    UNIQUE(fleet_id, pm_code, version)
   758→);
   759→
   760→-- Index for finding current PM type version
   761→CREATE INDEX idx_pm_types_current ON pm_types(fleet_id, pm_code, effective_from DESC)
   762→    WHERE effective_to IS NULL AND active = TRUE;
   763→
   764→-- Example PM hierarchy data:
   765→-- INSERT INTO pm_types (fleet_id, pm_code, pm_name, interval_km, interval_months, satisfies_codes, ...)
   766→-- KS Fleet:
   767→--   C1: interval_km=12500, interval_months=6,  satisfies_codes=NULL
   768→--   C2: interval_km=50000, interval_months=24, satisfies_codes=['C1']
   769→--   C3: interval_km=100000, interval_months=48, satisfies_codes=['C1','C2']
   770→--   C4: interval_km=200000, interval_months=96, satisfies_codes=['C1','C2','C3']
   771→--   OH: interval_km=800000, interval_months=NULL, satisfies_codes=['C1','C2','C3','C4']
   772→
   773→-- PM material requirements (Bill of Materials)
   774→CREATE TABLE pm_material_requirements (
   775→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   776→    pm_type_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES pm_types(id),
   777→    part_number VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
   778→    part_description TEXT,
   779→    
   780→    quantity_per_pm INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
   781→    criticality material_criticality NOT NULL DEFAULT 'BLOCKING',
   782→    -- BLOCKING = cannot start PM without part
   783→    -- DEFERRABLE = can start, must have within X days
   784→    -- CONSUMABLE = nice-to-have, can substitute
   785→    
   786→    lead_time_days INTEGER DEFAULT 14,

   788→    
   789→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),

   791→    UNIQUE(pm_type_id, part_number)

   794→CREATE INDEX idx_pm_materials_type ON pm_material_requirements(pm_type_id);
   795→
   796→-- PM schedule (planned and executed maintenance)
   797→CREATE TABLE pm_schedule (
   798→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),

   826→

   956→-- Man-hour projections (generated by leveling engine)
   957→CREATE TABLE manhour_projections (
   958→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   959→    projection_run_id UUID NOT NULL,
   960→    depot_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES depots(id),
   961→    
   962→    period_start DATE NOT NULL,
   963→    period_end DATE NOT NULL,
   964→    granularity VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,  -- 'WEEKLY', 'MONTHLY'
   965→

   967→    required_pm_hours DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
   968→    required_corrective_hours DECIMAL(10,2) DEFAULT 0,
   969→    required_overhaul_hours DECIMAL(10,2) DEFAULT 0,
   970→    required_total_hours DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
   971→    
   972→    -- Available hours
   973→    available_regular_hours DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
   974→    available_overtime_hours DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
   975→    available_total_hours DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
   976→    
   977→    -- Computed metrics
   978→    variance_hours DECIMAL(10,2) GENERATED ALWAYS AS 
   979→        (available_total_hours - required_total_hours) STORED,
   980→    utilization_pct DECIMAL(5,2) GENERATED ALWAYS AS 
   981→        (CASE WHEN available_total_hours > 0 
   982→              THEN (required_total_hours / available_total_hours * 100)

   984→    is_overloaded BOOLEAN GENERATED ALWAYS AS 
   985→        (required_total_hours > available_total_hours) STORED,
   986→    is_underutilized BOOLEAN GENERATED ALWAYS AS 
   987→        (required_total_hours < available_total_hours * 0.7) STORED,
   988→    
   989→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
   990→);
