  1219→);
  1220→```
  1221→
  1222→---
  1223→
  1224→## 3.10 Costing Tables
  1225→
  1226→```sql
  1227→-- Labor rates
  1228→CREATE TABLE labor_rates (
  1229→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  1230→    depot_id UUID REFERENCES depots(id),  -- NULL = global default
  1231→    effective_from DATE NOT NULL,
  1232→    effective_to DATE,
  1233→    
  1234→    -- Rate types
  1235→    regular_hourly_rate DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
  1236→    overtime_multiplier DECIMAL(3,2) DEFAULT 1.5,
  1237→    weekend_multiplier DECIMAL(3,2) DEFAULT 2.0,
  1238→    holiday_multiplier DECIMAL(3,2) DEFAULT 2.5,
  1239→    
  1240→    -- Shop rate (fully loaded)
  1241→    shop_rate_hourly DECIMAL(10,2),  -- Includes overhead, benefits
  1242→    
  1243→    currency VARCHAR(3) DEFAULT 'SGD',
  1244→    
  1245→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
  1246→    
  1247→    UNIQUE(depot_id, effective_from)
  1248→);
  1249→
  1250→-- Material cost history
  1251→CREATE TABLE material_costs (
  1252→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  1253→    part_number VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
  1254→    effective_from DATE NOT NULL,
  1255→    effective_to DATE,
  1256→    
  1257→    unit_cost DECIMAL(12,2) NOT NULL,
  1258→    currency VARCHAR(3) DEFAULT 'SGD',
  1259→    supplier VARCHAR(100),
  1260→    
  1261→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
  1262→    
  1263→    UNIQUE(part_number, effective_from)
  1264→);
  1265→
  1266→-- Cost baselines (for variance analysis)
  1267→CREATE TABLE cost_baselines (
  1268→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  1269→    fiscal_year INTEGER NOT NULL,
  1270→    depot_id UUID REFERENCES depots(id),
  1271→    
  1272→    budget_labor_hours DECIMAL(12,2),
  1273→    budget_labor_cost DECIMAL(14,2),
  1274→    budget_material_cost DECIMAL(14,2),
  1275→    budget_total DECIMAL(14,2),
  1276→    
  1277→    -- Actuals (updated monthly)
  1278→    actual_labor_hours DECIMAL(12,2),
  1279→    actual_labor_cost DECIMAL(14,2),
  1280→    actual_material_cost DECIMAL(14,2),
  1281→    actual_total DECIMAL(14,2),
  1282→    
  1283→    variance_pct DECIMAL(5,2) GENERATED ALWAYS AS 
  1284→        (CASE WHEN budget_total > 0 
  1285→              THEN ((actual_total - budget_total) / budget_total * 100)
  1286→              ELSE 0 END) STORED,
  1287→    
  1288→    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
  1289→    
  1290→    UNIQUE(fiscal_year, depot_id)
  1291→);
  1292→```
  1293→
  1294→---
  1295→
  1296→## 3.11 Audit & History Tables
  1297→
  1298→```sql
  1299→-- PM schedule change history (immutable audit trail)
  1300→CREATE TABLE pm_schedule_history (
  1301→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  1302→    pm_schedule_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES pm_schedule(id),
  1303→    
  1304→    change_type VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL,
  1305→    -- 'CREATED', 'DATE_CHANGED', 'STATUS_CHANGED', 'TRACK_ASSIGNED', 
  1306→    -- 'LOCKED', 'UNLOCKED', 'COMPLETED', 'CANCELLED', 'DEFERRED'
  1307→    
  1308→    -- Previous values
  1309→    old_target_date DATE,
  1310→    old_status pm_status,
  1311→    old_assigned_track_id UUID,
  1312→    old_assigned_hours DECIMAL(6,2),
  1313→    
  1314→    -- New values
  1315→    new_target_date DATE,
  1316→    new_status pm_status,
  1317→    new_assigned_track_id UUID,
  1318→    new_assigned_hours DECIMAL(6,2),
  1319→    
  1320→    -- Attribution
  1321→    changed_by UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
  1322→    changed_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
  1323→    change_reason TEXT,
  1324→    change_source VARCHAR(30) DEFAULT 'USER',  -- 'USER', 'OPTIMIZER', 'SYSTEM', 'IMPORT'
  1325→    
  1326→    -- Optimizer context (if change was suggested by optimizer)
  1327→    optimizer_request_id UUID,
  1328→    optimizer_explanation JSONB
  1329→);
  1330→
  1331→CREATE INDEX idx_pm_history_schedule ON pm_schedule_history(pm_schedule_id, changed_at DESC);
  1332→CREATE INDEX idx_pm_history_date ON pm_schedule_history(changed_at DESC);
  1333→CREATE INDEX idx_pm_history_user ON pm_schedule_history(changed_by, changed_at DESC);
  1334→
  1335→-- General audit log
  1336→CREATE TABLE audit_log (
  1337→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  1338→    
  1339→    -- What
  1340→    entity_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
  1341→    entity_id UUID NOT NULL,
  1342→    action VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL,  -- 'CREATE', 'UPDATE', 'DELETE', 'APPROVE', 'REJECT', 'PUBLISH'
  1343→    
  1344→    -- Who
  1345→    user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
  1346→    user_email VARCHAR(255),
  1347→    user_ip_address INET,
  1348→    
  1349→    -- When
  1350→    occurred_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
  1351→    
  1352→    -- Details
  1353→    old_values JSONB,
  1354→    new_values JSONB,
  1355→    metadata JSONB,  -- Additional context
  1356→    
  1357→    -- Compliance
  1358→    compliance_flags VARCHAR(50)[]  -- e.g., ['SAFETY_CRITICAL', 'FINANCIAL']
  1359→);
  1360→
  1361→CREATE INDEX idx_audit_entity ON audit_log(entity_type, entity_id, occurred_at DESC);
  1362→CREATE INDEX idx_audit_user ON audit_log(user_id, occurred_at DESC);
  1363→CREATE INDEX idx_audit_date ON audit_log(occurred_at DESC);
  1364→```
  1365→
  1366→---
  1367→
  1368→## 3.12 Users & Authentication Tables
  1369→
  1370→```sql
  1371→-- Users
  1372→CREATE TABLE users (
  1373→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  1374→    email VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
  1375→    password_hash VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  1376→    
  1377→    first_name VARCHAR(100),
  1378→    last_name VARCHAR(100),
  1379→    display_name VARCHAR(200),
  1380→    
  1381→    -- Assignment
  1382→    primary_depot_id UUID REFERENCES depots(id),
  1383→    role_ids UUID[] NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
  1384→    
  1385→    -- Certifications (for technician constraint matching)
  1386→    certifications VARCHAR(50)[],
  1387→    
  1388→    -- Status
  1389→    is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
  1390→    last_login_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  1391→    failed_login_attempts INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  1392→    locked_until TIMESTAMPTZ,
  1393→    
  1394→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
  1395→    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
  1396→);
  1397→
  1398→CREATE INDEX idx_users_email ON users(email);
  1399→CREATE INDEX idx_users_depot ON users(primary_depot_id) WHERE is_active = TRUE;
  1400→
  1401→-- Roles
  1402→CREATE TABLE roles (
  1403→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  1404→    name VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
  1405→    description TEXT,
  1406→    permissions JSONB NOT NULL,  -- e.g., {"pm_schedule": ["read", "write"], "baseline": ["read"]}
  1407→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
  1408→);
  1409→
  1410→-- Sessions (Redis-backed, but schema for reference)
  1411→CREATE TABLE sessions (
  1412→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  1413→    user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
  1414→    token_hash VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  1415→    
  1416→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
  1417→    expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
  1418→    last_activity_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
  1419→    
  1420→    ip_address INET,
  1421→    user_agent TEXT
  1422→);
  1423→
  1424→CREATE INDEX idx_sessions_user ON sessions(user_id);
  1425→CREATE INDEX idx_sessions_expiry ON sessions(expires_at);
  1426→```
  1427→
  1428→---
  1429→
  1430→## 3.13 Canvas-Optimized Views
  1431→
  1432→```sql
  1433→-- Strategic zoom level (yearly aggregates)
  1434→CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW v_canvas_strategic AS
  1435→SELECT 
  1436→    t.id as train_id,
  1437→    t.train_number,
  1438→    t.fleet_id,
  1439→    date_trunc('year', ps.target_date) as year,
  1440→    COUNT(*) as pm_count,
  1441→    SUM(pt.estimated_hours) as total_hours,
  1442→    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE ps.status = 'COMPLETED') as completed_count,
  1443→    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE ps.is_locked) as locked_count,
  1444→    SUM(pt.estimated_hours) FILTER (WHERE ps.status = 'SCHEDULED') as pending_hours
  1445→FROM trains t
  1446→LEFT JOIN pm_schedule ps ON t.id = ps.train_id AND ps.scenario_id IS NULL
  1447→LEFT JOIN pm_types pt ON ps.pm_type_id = pt.id
  1448→WHERE t.active = TRUE
  1449→GROUP BY t.id, t.train_number, t.fleet_id, date_trunc('year', ps.target_date);
  1450→
  1451→CREATE INDEX idx_v_canvas_strategic_train ON v_canvas_strategic(train_id, year);
  1452→
  1453→-- Tactical zoom level (weekly aggregates with PM details)
  1454→CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW v_canvas_tactical AS
  1455→SELECT 
  1456→    t.id as train_id,
  1457→    t.train_number,
  1458→    date_trunc('week', ps.target_date) as week_start,
  1459→    ps.id as pm_id,
  1460→    ps.target_date,
  1461→    ps.status,
  1462→    ps.constraint_start,
  1463→    ps.constraint_end,
  1464→    ps.is_locked,
  1465→    pt.pm_code,
  1466→    pt.estimated_hours,
  1467→    pt.display_color,
  1468→    tr.track_number,
  1469→    d.code as depot_code
  1470→FROM trains t
  1471→JOIN pm_schedule ps ON t.id = ps.train_id AND ps.scenario_id IS NULL
  1472→JOIN pm_types pt ON ps.pm_type_id = pt.id
  1473→LEFT JOIN tracks tr ON ps.assigned_track_id = tr.id
  1474→LEFT JOIN depots d ON ps.assigned_depot_id = d.id
  1475→WHERE t.active = TRUE
  1476→  AND ps.status IN ('SCHEDULED', 'IN_PROGRESS');
  1477→
  1478→CREATE INDEX idx_v_canvas_tactical_train ON v_canvas_tactical(train_id, week_start);
  1479→CREATE INDEX idx_v_canvas_tactical_date ON v_canvas_tactical(target_date);
  1480→
  1481→-- Man-hour canvas overlay
  1482→CREATE VIEW v_manhour_canvas AS
  1483→SELECT 
  1484→    mp.depot_id,
  1485→    d.code as depot_code,
  1486→    mp.period_start,
  1487→    mp.period_end,
  1488→    mp.required_total_hours,
  1489→    mp.available_total_hours,
  1490→    mp.variance_hours,
  1491→    mp.utilization_pct,
  1492→    mp.is_overloaded,
  1493→    mp.is_underutilized,
  1494→    COALESCE(adj.adjustment_count, 0) as pending_adjustments,
  1495→    COALESCE(adj.total_adjustment_hours, 0) as proposed_adjustment_hours
  1496→FROM manhour_projections mp
  1497→JOIN depots d ON mp.depot_id = d.id
  1498→LEFT JOIN (
  1499→    SELECT 
  1500→        projection_id,
  1501→        COUNT(*) as adjustment_count,
  1502→        SUM(hours_adjusted) as total_adjustment_hours
  1503→    FROM manhour_adjustments
  1504→    WHERE status = 'PROPOSED'
  1505→    GROUP BY projection_id
  1506→) adj ON mp.id = adj.projection_id
  1507→WHERE mp.projection_run_id = (
  1508→    SELECT projection_run_id 
  1509→    FROM manhour_projections 
  1510→    ORDER BY created_at DESC 
  1511→    LIMIT 1
  1512→);
  1513→
  1514→-- Drift overlay (comparison to baseline)
  1515→CREATE VIEW v_drift_canvas AS
  1516→SELECT 
  1517→    da.train_id,
  1518→    t.train_number,
  1519→    da.baseline_id,
  1520→    pb.name as baseline_name,
  1521→    da.analysis_date,
  1522→    da.mileage_drift_km,
  1523→    da.mileage_drift_pct,
  1524→    da.avg_pm_drift_days,
  1525→    da.requires_replan,
  1526→    da.replan_reason,
  1527→    -- Color coding for canvas
  1528→    CASE 
  1529→        WHEN da.requires_replan THEN 'RED'
  1530→        WHEN ABS(da.mileage_drift_pct) > 3 THEN 'YELLOW'
  1531→        ELSE 'GREEN'
  1532→    END as drift_severity
  1533→FROM drift_analysis da
  1534→JOIN trains t ON da.train_id = t.id
  1535→JOIN plan_baselines pb ON da.baseline_id = pb.id
  1536→WHERE pb.status = 'PUBLISHED'
  1537→  AND da.analysis_date = (
  1538→      SELECT MAX(analysis_date) 
  1539→      FROM drift_analysis 
  1540→      WHERE baseline_id = da.baseline_id
  1541→  );
  1542→
  1543→-- Refresh materialized views (scheduled job)
  1544→-- REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY v_canvas_strategic;
  1545→-- REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY v_canvas_tactical;
  1546→```
  1547→
  1548→---
  1549→
  1550→## 3.14 Database Maintenance
  1551→
  1552→```sql
  1553→-- Automatic refresh of materialized views
  1554→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION refresh_canvas_views()
  1555→RETURNS void AS $$
  1556→BEGIN
  1557→    REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY v_canvas_strategic;
  1558→    REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY v_canvas_tactical;
  1559→END;
  1560→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
  1561→
  1562→-- Schedule refresh every 15 minutes (use pg_cron or external scheduler)
  1563→-- SELECT cron.schedule('*/15 * * * *', 'SELECT refresh_canvas_views()');
  1564→
  1565→-- Cleanup expired scenarios
  1566→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION cleanup_expired_scenarios()
  1567→RETURNS INTEGER AS $$
  1568→DECLARE
  1569→    deleted_count INTEGER;
  1570→BEGIN
  1571→    UPDATE scenarios 
  1572→    SET status = 'EXPIRED'
  1573→    WHERE status = 'ACTIVE' 
  1574→      AND expires_at < NOW();
  1575→    
  1576→    GET DIAGNOSTICS deleted_count = ROW_COUNT;
  1577→    RETURN deleted_count;
  1578→END;
  1579→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
  1580→
  1581→-- Release expired soft reservations
  1582→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION release_expired_reservations()
  1583→RETURNS INTEGER AS $$
  1584→DECLARE
  1585→    released_count INTEGER;
  1586→BEGIN
  1587→    -- Update inventory positions
  1588→    UPDATE inventory_positions ip
  1589→    SET quantity_reserved = quantity_reserved - expired.total_qty
  1590→    FROM (
  1591→        SELECT inventory_position_id, SUM(quantity_reserved) as total_qty
  1592→        FROM material_reservations
  1593→        WHERE reservation_type = 'SOFT'
  1594→          AND expires_at < NOW()
  1595→          AND fulfilled_at IS NULL
  1596→        GROUP BY inventory_position_id
  1597→    ) expired
  1598→    WHERE ip.id = expired.inventory_position_id;
  1599→    
  1600→    -- Delete expired reservations
  1601→    DELETE FROM material_reservations
  1602→    WHERE reservation_type = 'SOFT'
  1603→      AND expires_at < NOW()
  1604→      AND fulfilled_at IS NULL;
  1605→    
  1606→    GET DIAGNOSTICS released_count = ROW_COUNT;
  1607→    RETURN released_count;
  1608→END;
  1609→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
  1610→
  1611→-- Update train target mileage for fleet balancing
  1612→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION update_fleet_mileage_targets()
  1613→RETURNS void AS $$
  1614→BEGIN
  1615→    UPDATE trains t
  1616→    SET target_mileage_km = fleet_avg.avg_mileage
  1617→    FROM (
  1618→        SELECT fleet_id, AVG(current_mileage_km)::INTEGER as avg_mileage
  1619→        FROM trains
  1620→        WHERE active = TRUE
  1621→        GROUP BY fleet_id
  1622→    ) fleet_avg
  1623→    WHERE t.fleet_id = fleet_avg.fleet_id
  1624→      AND t.active = TRUE;
  1625→END;
  1626→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
  1627→```

# Metro Fleet Mileage Management System
# Part 3: Database Schema

**Document Version:** 2.1  
**Status:** APPROVED FOR DEVELOPMENT  
**Last Updated:** 2026-01-19

---

## 3.1 Schema Overview

The database schema is organized into the following domains:

| Domain | Tables | Purpose |
|--------|--------|---------|
| Core Fleet | `fleets`, `trains`, `depots`, `routes` | Asset master data |
| **Operational Constraints** | `service_demand`, `route_utilization_profiles`, `train_route_compatibility` | **Operational requirements and constraints** |
| **Unavailability** | `unavailability_events` | **Corrective maintenance and incident tracking** |
| Mileage Tracking | `mileage_readings`, `meter_events` | Time-series odometer data |
| Component Tracking | `train_components`, `component_mileage` | Component-level lifecycle |
| PM Management | `pm_types`, `pm_schedule`, `pm_material_requirements` | Maintenance planning |
| **Track Capabilities** | `track_capabilities`, `pm_capability_requirements` | **Qualified resource matching** |
| **Data Migration** | `pm_baseline_assumptions` | **Cold start and history inference** |
| Material Management | `inventory_positions`, `material_reservations` | Parts availability |
| Man-hour Planning | `manhour_capacity`, `manhour_projections`, `manhour_adjustments` | Workforce balancing |
| Baseline & Drift | `plan_baselines`, `baseline_pm_schedule`, `baseline_mileage_projection` | Plan stability |
| Scenario Management | `scenarios`, `scenario_deltas` | What-if analysis |
| Costing | `labor_rates`, `material_costs`, `cost_baselines` | Financial tracking |
| Audit & History | `pm_schedule_history`, `audit_log` | Change tracking |
| Users & Auth | `users`, `roles`, `sessions` | Access control |

> **v2.1 Changes:** Added operational constraints domain (service demand, route utilization, train-route compatibility), unavailability events, track capabilities with PM requirements, and data migration assumptions tables.

---

## 3.2 Core Fleet Tables

```sql
-- Enable required extensions
CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS "uuid-ossp";
CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS timescaledb;

-- Enumerated types
CREATE TYPE fitness_status AS ENUM ('FIT', 'RESTRICTED', 'GROUNDED');
CREATE TYPE pm_status AS ENUM ('SCHEDULED', 'IN_PROGRESS', 'COMPLETED', 'CANCELLED', 'DEFERRED');
CREATE TYPE track_type AS ENUM ('LIGHT', 'HEAVY', 'OVERHAUL', 'STABLING');
CREATE TYPE shift_type AS ENUM ('DAY', 'NIGHT', 'WEEKEND', 'HOLIDAY');
CREATE TYPE adjustment_status AS ENUM ('PROPOSED', 'APPROVED', 'REJECTED', 'APPLIED');
CREATE TYPE baseline_status AS ENUM ('DRAFT', 'PUBLISHED', 'SUPERSEDED', 'ARCHIVED');
CREATE TYPE scenario_status AS ENUM ('ACTIVE', 'PROMOTED', 'DISCARDED', 'EXPIRED');
CREATE TYPE material_criticality AS ENUM ('BLOCKING', 'DEFERRABLE', 'CONSUMABLE');
CREATE TYPE reservation_type AS ENUM ('SOFT', 'HARD');

-- NEW: v2.1 enum types
CREATE TYPE day_type AS ENUM ('WEEKDAY', 'SATURDAY', 'SUNDAY', 'HOLIDAY');
CREATE TYPE unavailability_type AS ENUM ('CORRECTIVE', 'INCIDENT', 'COLLISION', 'VANDALISM', 'EXTERNAL', 'PLANNED_OUTAGE');
CREATE TYPE unavailability_status AS ENUM ('ACTIVE', 'RESOLVED');
CREATE TYPE track_capability AS ENUM ('PIT', 'CRANE', 'LIFTING_JACKS', 'WHEEL_LATHE', 'CATENARY_ISOLATION', 'ROOF_ACCESS', 'WASH', 'DROP_TABLE');
CREATE TYPE constraint_type AS ENUM ('SIGNALING', 'GAUGE', 'POWER_SUPPLY', 'PLATFORM_LENGTH', 'MAX_SPEED', 'TUNNEL_CLEARANCE');
CREATE TYPE assumption_type AS ENUM ('INFERRED_FROM_COMMISSIONING', 'INFERRED_FROM_HIGHER_PM', 'MANUAL_OVERRIDE', 'IMPORTED', 'NO_HISTORY');
CREATE TYPE confidence_level AS ENUM ('HIGH', 'MEDIUM', 'LOW', 'ESTIMATED');

-- Depots
CREATE TABLE depots (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    code VARCHAR(10) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    location VARCHAR(200),
    timezone VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT 'Asia/Singapore',
    active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- Fleets (train types/manufacturers)
CREATE TABLE fleets (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    code VARCHAR(10) NOT NULL UNIQUE,  -- 'KS', 'ALSTOM'
    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    manufacturer VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    model VARCHAR(100),
    base_depot_id UUID REFERENCES depots(id),
    cars_per_train INTEGER DEFAULT 6,
    active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- Trains
CREATE TABLE trains (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    fleet_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES fleets(id),
    train_number VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    commissioning_date DATE,
    current_mileage_km INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    fitness_status fitness_status DEFAULT 'FIT',
    fitness_notes TEXT,
    current_depot_id UUID REFERENCES depots(id),
    home_depot_id UUID REFERENCES depots(id),
    active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
    
    -- For mileage balancing
    target_mileage_km INTEGER,  -- Calculated target for fleet balance
    mileage_variance_km INTEGER GENERATED ALWAYS AS 
        (current_mileage_km - COALESCE(target_mileage_km, current_mileage_km)) STORED,
    
    -- Canvas display properties
    display_color VARCHAR(7),
    display_order INTEGER,
    
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_trains_fleet ON trains(fleet_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_trains_fitness ON trains(fitness_status) WHERE active = TRUE;
CREATE INDEX idx_trains_depot ON trains(current_depot_id);

-- Routes
CREATE TABLE routes (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    route_code VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    route_name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    line_id VARCHAR(20),
    expected_km_per_day INTEGER NOT NULL,
    required_trains INTEGER NOT NULL,
    
    -- NEW v2.1: Route characteristics for compatibility matching
    default_daily_km INTEGER,                        -- Default mileage for projection
    signaling_system VARCHAR(30),                    -- 'ATC', 'CBTC', 'ERTMS', 'LEGACY'
    power_system VARCHAR(30),                        -- 'THIRD_RAIL_750V', 'OVERHEAD_25KV', 'OVERHEAD_1500V'
    tunnel_clearance_mm INTEGER,                     -- Maximum vehicle width allowed
    platform_length_m DECIMAL(5,2),                  -- Platform length constraint
```

## 3.2.3 Unavailability Events (NEW v2.1)

Unavailability events track trains that cannot be scheduled due to corrective maintenance, incidents, or other blocking factors. This is a **critical integration point** with Maximo CMMS.

```sql
-- Unavailability events (corrective maintenance, incidents, etc.)
CREATE TABLE unavailability_events (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    train_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES trains(id),
    
    -- Event classification
    event_type unavailability_type NOT NULL,  -- 'CORRECTIVE', 'INCIDENT', etc.
    status unavailability_status NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ACTIVE',
    restriction_level fitness_status NOT NULL, -- Uses existing ENUM: 'RESTRICTED' or 'GROUNDED'
    
    -- Timing
    reported_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    estimated_repair_hours INTEGER,
    estimated_return_date DATE,
    actual_return_date DATE,
    
    -- External system linkage
    source_system VARCHAR(30),        -- 'MAXIMO', 'OCC', 'MANUAL'
    external_reference VARCHAR(50),   -- Maximo WO number (e.g., 'WO-2026-001234')
    external_status VARCHAR(30),      -- Maximo status (e.g., 'INPRG', 'WAPPR')
    
    -- Description
    description TEXT,
    failure_code VARCHAR(30),         -- Standard failure classification
    affected_system VARCHAR(50),      -- 'TRACTION', 'BRAKES', 'DOORS', 'HVAC', etc.
    
    -- Scheduling impact flags
    blocks_pm_scheduling BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
    blocks_run_assignment BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
    
    -- Resolution
    resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    resolved_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
    resolution_notes TEXT,
    
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    
    -- Constraint: Cannot have resolved data if status is ACTIVE
    CONSTRAINT chk_resolved_status CHECK (
        (status = 'ACTIVE' AND resolved_at IS NULL) OR
        (status = 'RESOLVED' AND resolved_at IS NOT NULL)
    )
);

-- Primary query: Find active unavailability for a train
CREATE INDEX idx_unavailability_active ON unavailability_events(train_id, status) 
    WHERE status = 'ACTIVE';
-- Dashboard query: All active unavailability by estimated return
CREATE INDEX idx_unavailability_return ON unavailability_events(estimated_return_date) 
    WHERE status = 'ACTIVE';
-- Maximo sync: Find by external reference
CREATE INDEX idx_unavailability_external ON unavailability_events(source_system, external_reference);
-- Audit: Find by type and date range
CREATE INDEX idx_unavailability_audit ON unavailability_events(event_type, reported_at DESC);

-- Trigger to update train fitness_status when unavailability changes
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION sync_train_fitness_from_unavailability()
RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
BEGIN
    IF TG_OP = 'INSERT' OR TG_OP = 'UPDATE' THEN
        IF NEW.status = 'ACTIVE' THEN
            UPDATE trains 
            SET fitness_status = NEW.restriction_level,
                fitness_notes = COALESCE(NEW.description, 'Unavailability event: ' || NEW.event_type),
                updated_at = NOW()
            WHERE id = NEW.train_id;
        ELSIF NEW.status = 'RESOLVED' THEN
            -- Only reset to FIT if no other active unavailability exists
            IF NOT EXISTS (
                SELECT 1 FROM unavailability_events 
                WHERE train_id = NEW.train_id 
                AND status = 'ACTIVE' 
                AND id != NEW.id
            ) THEN
                UPDATE trains 
                SET fitness_status = 'FIT',
                    fitness_notes = NULL,
                    updated_at = NOW()
                WHERE id = NEW.train_id;
            END IF;
        END IF;
    END IF;
    RETURN NEW;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

CREATE TRIGGER trg_sync_train_fitness
AFTER INSERT OR UPDATE ON unavailability_events
FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION sync_train_fitness_from_unavailability();
```

## 3.2.4 Train-Route Compatibility (NEW v2.1)

Defines which trains can operate on which routes based on technical constraints (signaling, gauge, power supply, etc.). Prevents the optimizer from assigning trains to incompatible routes.

```sql
-- Train-route compatibility matrix
CREATE TABLE train_route_compatibility (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    
    -- Can be at train or fleet level
    train_id UUID REFERENCES trains(id),
    fleet_id UUID REFERENCES fleets(id),
    route_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES routes(id),
    
    -- Compatibility determination
    is_compatible BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE,
    constraint_type constraint_type,   -- 'SIGNALING', 'GAUGE', 'POWER_SUPPLY', etc.
    restriction_reason TEXT,           -- Human-readable explanation
    
    -- Validity period (for temporary restrictions)
    effective_date DATE DEFAULT CURRENT_DATE,
    expiry_date DATE,                  -- NULL = permanent
    
    -- Metadata
    verified_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
    verified_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    notes TEXT,
    
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    
    -- Must specify either train_id OR fleet_id, not both
    CONSTRAINT chk_train_or_fleet CHECK (
        (train_id IS NOT NULL AND fleet_id IS NULL) OR
        (train_id IS NULL AND fleet_id IS NOT NULL)
    )
);

CREATE INDEX idx_compatibility_route ON train_route_compatibility(route_id, is_compatible);
CREATE INDEX idx_compatibility_train ON train_route_compatibility(train_id, is_compatible) 
    WHERE train_id IS NOT NULL;
CREATE INDEX idx_compatibility_fleet ON train_route_compatibility(fleet_id, is_compatible) 
    WHERE fleet_id IS NOT NULL;
CREATE INDEX idx_compatibility_active ON train_route_compatibility(effective_date, expiry_date)
    WHERE is_compatible = FALSE;
```

## 3.2.6 PM Baseline Assumptions (NEW v2.1)

Handles cold start scenarios where PM history is incomplete during data migration. Tracks assumptions with confidence levels and flags records requiring manual review.

```sql
-- PM history baseline assumptions for cold start scenarios
CREATE TABLE pm_baseline_assumptions (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    train_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES trains(id),
    pm_type_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES pm_types(id),
    
    -- Assumption details

    -- 'INFERRED_FROM_COMMISSIONING' - assume first PM at commissioning date + interval
    -- 'INFERRED_FROM_HIGHER_PM' - derived from C4/OH completion implies C1/C2/C3 done
    -- 'MANUAL_OVERRIDE' - operator entered value
    -- 'IMPORTED' - from legacy system with known quality
    -- 'NO_HISTORY' - no data available, using conservative estimate
    
    -- Assumed baseline values
    assumed_last_pm_date DATE,
    assumed_last_pm_mileage INTEGER,
    confidence_level confidence_level NOT NULL,  -- 'HIGH', 'MEDIUM', 'LOW', 'ESTIMATED'
    
    -- Inference source documentation
    inference_source TEXT,             -- Explanation of how assumption was derived
    inference_data JSONB,              -- Supporting data (e.g., higher PM dates)
    
    -- Review workflow
    requires_review BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
    reviewed_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
    reviewed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    review_notes TEXT,
    
    -- When applied
    applied_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    applied_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
    
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    
    UNIQUE(train_id, pm_type_id)
);

    WHERE requires_review = TRUE;

    WHERE confidence_level IN ('LOW', 'ESTIMATED');

-- Example inference rules (implemented in application logic):
-- 1. If train commissioned 2020-01-01 and C1 interval is 6 months:
```

```sql
-- Meter events (odometer replacements, resets, swaps)
CREATE TABLE meter_events (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    train_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES trains(id),
    event_date DATE NOT NULL,
    event_type VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL,  -- 'REPLACEMENT', 'RESET', 'CALIBRATION', 'SWAP'
    
    old_meter_serial VARCHAR(50),
    new_meter_serial VARCHAR(50),
    old_meter_reading INTEGER,
    new_meter_reading INTEGER,
    adjustment_km INTEGER,  -- Cumulative mileage adjustment to apply
    
    reason TEXT,

    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    created_by UUID REFERENCES users(id)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_meter_events_train ON meter_events(train_id, event_date DESC);

-- Daily mileage readings (TimescaleDB hypertable)
CREATE TABLE mileage_readings (
    train_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES trains(id),
    reading_date DATE NOT NULL,
    
    -- Raw and adjusted values
    raw_reading INTEGER NOT NULL,        -- Odometer value from CSV
    adjusted_reading INTEGER NOT NULL,   -- After meter event adjustments
    daily_km INTEGER,                    -- Calculated: today - yesterday
    
    -- Validation
    validation_status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'PENDING',  -- 'PENDING', 'VALID', 'ANOMALY', 'CORRECTED'
    anomaly_type VARCHAR(30),            -- 'NEGATIVE', 'SPIKE', 'MISSING', 'DUPLICATE'
    anomaly_notes TEXT,
    
    -- Source tracking
    source_file VARCHAR(255),
    meter_event_id UUID REFERENCES meter_events(id),
    
    ingested_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    
    PRIMARY KEY (train_id, reading_date)
);

-- Convert to TimescaleDB hypertable
SELECT create_hypertable('mileage_readings', 'reading_date', 
    chunk_time_interval => INTERVAL '7 days',
    if_not_exists => TRUE
);

-- Compression policy (compress data older than 30 days)
SELECT add_compression_policy('mileage_readings', INTERVAL '30 days');

-- Retention policy (keep 13 years for contract + buffer)
SELECT add_retention_policy('mileage_readings', INTERVAL '13 years');

-- Indexes for common queries
CREATE INDEX idx_mileage_train_date ON mileage_readings (train_id, reading_date DESC);
CREATE INDEX idx_mileage_validation ON mileage_readings (validation_status, reading_date DESC) 
    WHERE validation_status != 'VALID';

-- Continuous aggregate for dashboard queries
CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW daily_fleet_mileage
WITH (timescaledb.continuous) AS
SELECT 
    time_bucket('1 day', reading_date) AS bucket,
    t.fleet_id,
    COUNT(*) as train_count,
    SUM(daily_km) as total_daily_km,
    AVG(daily_km) as avg_daily_km,
    MIN(daily_km) as min_daily_km,
    MAX(daily_km) as max_daily_km,
    STDDEV(daily_km) as stddev_daily_km
FROM mileage_readings mr
JOIN trains t ON mr.train_id = t.id
WHERE mr.validation_status = 'VALID'
GROUP BY bucket, t.fleet_id
WITH NO DATA;

SELECT add_continuous_aggregate_policy('daily_fleet_mileage',
    start_offset => INTERVAL '3 days',
    end_offset => INTERVAL '1 hour',
    schedule_interval => INTERVAL '1 hour'
);
```

```sql
    satisfies_codes VARCHAR(20)[],

    track_type track_type NOT NULL,

    early_tolerance_pct DECIMAL(5,2) DEFAULT 10.0,   -- Can do 10% early

    required_certifications VARCHAR(50)[],
    
    -- NEW v2.1: PM type versioning for interval changes over contract life

    superseded_by UUID REFERENCES pm_types(id),    -- Link to newer version

    display_priority INTEGER DEFAULT 50,

-- PM schedule (planned and executed maintenance)
CREATE TABLE pm_schedule (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    train_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES trains(id),
    pm_type_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES pm_types(id),
    
    -- Scheduling
    target_date DATE NOT NULL,
    target_mileage_km INTEGER,
    status pm_status DEFAULT 'SCHEDULED',
    
    -- Compliance window (calculated from pm_types tolerances)
    constraint_start DATE NOT NULL,    -- Earliest allowed date
    constraint_end DATE NOT NULL,      -- Latest allowed date (statutory lock after this)
    
    -- Resource assignment
    assigned_depot_id UUID REFERENCES depots(id),
    assigned_track_id UUID REFERENCES tracks(id),
    assigned_hours DECIMAL(6,2),
    assigned_shift shift_type,
    
    -- Execution
    actual_start_date DATE,
    actual_end_date DATE,
    actual_hours DECIMAL(6,2),
    completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    completed_mileage_km INTEGER,
    
    -- Work order integration
    maximo_wo_id VARCHAR(50),
    
    -- Locking
    is_locked BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,   -- Cannot be moved by optimizer
    locked_reason VARCHAR(100),
    locked_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
    locked_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    
    -- Scenario support (NULL = production schedule)
    scenario_id UUID REFERENCES scenarios(id),
    
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    created_by UUID REFERENCES users(id)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_pm_schedule_train ON pm_schedule(train_id, target_date);
CREATE INDEX idx_pm_schedule_date ON pm_schedule(target_date, status);
CREATE INDEX idx_pm_schedule_depot ON pm_schedule(assigned_depot_id, target_date) 
    WHERE status IN ('SCHEDULED', 'IN_PROGRESS');
CREATE INDEX idx_pm_schedule_scenario ON pm_schedule(scenario_id) WHERE scenario_id IS NOT NULL;
CREATE INDEX idx_pm_schedule_canvas ON pm_schedule(target_date, train_id)
    WHERE status IN ('SCHEDULED', 'IN_PROGRESS') AND scenario_id IS NULL;
```

## 3.7 Man-hour Planning Tables

```sql
-- Man-hour capacity (resource availability by depot)
CREATE TABLE manhour_capacity (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    depot_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES depots(id),
    effective_from DATE NOT NULL,
    effective_to DATE,
    
    -- Base capacity per shift type
    day_shift_hours DECIMAL(8,2) NOT NULL,
    night_shift_hours DECIMAL(8,2) DEFAULT 0,
    weekend_hours DECIMAL(8,2) DEFAULT 0,
    
    -- Staff counts
    technicians_day INTEGER NOT NULL,
    technicians_night INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    specialists INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    
    -- Constraints
    max_overtime_pct DECIMAL(5,2) DEFAULT 20.0,
    min_utilization_pct DECIMAL(5,2) DEFAULT 70.0,
    target_utilization_pct DECIMAL(5,2) DEFAULT 85.0,
    
    notes TEXT,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    
    UNIQUE(depot_id, effective_from)
);

-- Man-hour projections (generated by leveling engine)
CREATE TABLE manhour_projections (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    projection_run_id UUID NOT NULL,
    depot_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES depots(id),
    
    period_start DATE NOT NULL,
    period_end DATE NOT NULL,
    granularity VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,  -- 'WEEKLY', 'MONTHLY'
    
    -- Required hours breakdown
    required_pm_hours DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
    required_corrective_hours DECIMAL(10,2) DEFAULT 0,
    required_overhaul_hours DECIMAL(10,2) DEFAULT 0,
    required_total_hours DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
    
    -- Available hours
    available_regular_hours DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
    available_overtime_hours DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
    available_total_hours DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
    
    -- Computed metrics
    variance_hours DECIMAL(10,2) GENERATED ALWAYS AS 
        (available_total_hours - required_total_hours) STORED,
    utilization_pct DECIMAL(5,2) GENERATED ALWAYS AS 
        (CASE WHEN available_total_hours > 0 
              THEN (required_total_hours / available_total_hours * 100)
              ELSE 0 END) STORED,
    is_overloaded BOOLEAN GENERATED ALWAYS AS 
        (required_total_hours > available_total_hours) STORED,
    is_underutilized BOOLEAN GENERATED ALWAYS AS 
        (required_total_hours < available_total_hours * 0.7) STORED,
    
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_manhour_proj_depot ON manhour_projections(depot_id, period_start);
CREATE INDEX idx_manhour_proj_run ON manhour_projections(projection_run_id);

-- Man-hour adjustments (proposed changes to level workload)
CREATE TABLE manhour_adjustments (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    projection_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES manhour_projections(id),
    
    adjustment_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    -- 'ADD_OVERTIME', 'DEFER_PM', 'ADVANCE_PM', 'CROSS_DEPOT', 'ADD_CONTRACTOR'
    
    source_period_start DATE,
    source_period_end DATE,
    target_period_start DATE,
    target_period_end DATE,
    hours_adjusted DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
    
    affected_pm_ids UUID[] DEFAULT '{}',
    affected_train_ids UUID[] DEFAULT '{}',
    
    -- Impact assessment
    cost_impact DECIMAL(12,2),
    risk_impact VARCHAR(20),  -- 'NONE', 'LOW', 'MEDIUM', 'HIGH'
    risk_description TEXT,
    
    -- Workflow
    status adjustment_status DEFAULT 'PROPOSED',
    proposed_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
    proposed_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    approved_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
    approved_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    rejection_reason TEXT,
    
    -- Explanation
    reason TEXT NOT NULL,
    
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_manhour_adj_projection ON manhour_adjustments(projection_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_manhour_adj_status ON manhour_adjustments(status) WHERE status = 'PROPOSED';
```

## 3.13 Canvas-Optimized Views

```sql
-- Strategic zoom level (yearly aggregates)
CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW v_canvas_strategic AS
SELECT 
    t.id as train_id,
    t.train_number,
    t.fleet_id,
    date_trunc('year', ps.target_date) as year,
    COUNT(*) as pm_count,
    SUM(pt.estimated_hours) as total_hours,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE ps.status = 'COMPLETED') as completed_count,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE ps.is_locked) as locked_count,
    SUM(pt.estimated_hours) FILTER (WHERE ps.status = 'SCHEDULED') as pending_hours
FROM trains t
LEFT JOIN pm_schedule ps ON t.id = ps.train_id AND ps.scenario_id IS NULL
LEFT JOIN pm_types pt ON ps.pm_type_id = pt.id
WHERE t.active = TRUE
GROUP BY t.id, t.train_number, t.fleet_id, date_trunc('year', ps.target_date);

CREATE INDEX idx_v_canvas_strategic_train ON v_canvas_strategic(train_id, year);

-- Tactical zoom level (weekly aggregates with PM details)
CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW v_canvas_tactical AS
SELECT 
    t.id as train_id,
    t.train_number,
    date_trunc('week', ps.target_date) as week_start,
    ps.id as pm_id,
    ps.target_date,
    ps.status,
    ps.constraint_start,
    ps.constraint_end,
    ps.is_locked,
    pt.pm_code,
    pt.estimated_hours,
    pt.display_color,
    tr.track_number,
    d.code as depot_code
FROM trains t
JOIN pm_schedule ps ON t.id = ps.train_id AND ps.scenario_id IS NULL
JOIN pm_types pt ON ps.pm_type_id = pt.id
LEFT JOIN tracks tr ON ps.assigned_track_id = tr.id
LEFT JOIN depots d ON ps.assigned_depot_id = d.id
WHERE t.active = TRUE
  AND ps.status IN ('SCHEDULED', 'IN_PROGRESS');

CREATE INDEX idx_v_canvas_tactical_train ON v_canvas_tactical(train_id, week_start);
CREATE INDEX idx_v_canvas_tactical_date ON v_canvas_tactical(target_date);

-- Man-hour canvas overlay
CREATE VIEW v_manhour_canvas AS
SELECT 
    mp.depot_id,
    d.code as depot_code,
    mp.period_start,
    mp.period_end,
    mp.required_total_hours,
    mp.available_total_hours,
    mp.variance_hours,
    mp.utilization_pct,
    mp.is_overloaded,
    mp.is_underutilized,
    COALESCE(adj.adjustment_count, 0) as pending_adjustments,
    COALESCE(adj.total_adjustment_hours, 0) as proposed_adjustment_hours
FROM manhour_projections mp
JOIN depots d ON mp.depot_id = d.id
LEFT JOIN (
    SELECT 
        projection_id,
        COUNT(*) as adjustment_count,
        SUM(hours_adjusted) as total_adjustment_hours
    FROM manhour_adjustments
    WHERE status = 'PROPOSED'
    GROUP BY projection_id
) adj ON mp.id = adj.projection_id
WHERE mp.projection_run_id = (
    SELECT projection_run_id 
    FROM manhour_projections 
    ORDER BY created_at DESC 
    LIMIT 1
);

-- Drift overlay (comparison to baseline)
CREATE VIEW v_drift_canvas AS
SELECT 
    da.train_id,
    t.train_number,
    da.baseline_id,
    pb.name as baseline_name,
    da.analysis_date,
    da.mileage_drift_km,
    da.mileage_drift_pct,
    da.avg_pm_drift_days,
    da.requires_replan,
    da.replan_reason,
    -- Color coding for canvas
    CASE 
        WHEN da.requires_replan THEN 'RED'
        WHEN ABS(da.mileage_drift_pct) > 3 THEN 'YELLOW'
        ELSE 'GREEN'
    END as drift_severity
FROM drift_analysis da
JOIN trains t ON da.train_id = t.id
JOIN plan_baselines pb ON da.baseline_id = pb.id
WHERE pb.status = 'PUBLISHED'
  AND da.analysis_date = (
      SELECT MAX(analysis_date) 
      FROM drift_analysis 
      WHERE baseline_id = da.baseline_id
  );

-- Refresh materialized views (scheduled job)
-- REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY v_canvas_strategic;
-- REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY v_canvas_tactical;
```

---

## 3.14 Database Maintenance

```sql
-- Automatic refresh of materialized views
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION refresh_canvas_views()
RETURNS void AS $$
BEGIN
    REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY v_canvas_strategic;
    REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY v_canvas_tactical;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

-- Schedule refresh every 15 minutes (use pg_cron or external scheduler)
-- SELECT cron.schedule('*/15 * * * *', 'SELECT refresh_canvas_views()');

-- Cleanup expired scenarios
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION cleanup_expired_scenarios()
RETURNS INTEGER AS $$
DECLARE
    deleted_count INTEGER;
BEGIN
    UPDATE scenarios 
    SET status = 'EXPIRED'
    WHERE status = 'ACTIVE' 
      AND expires_at < NOW();
    
    GET DIAGNOSTICS deleted_count = ROW_COUNT;
    RETURN deleted_count;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

-- Release expired soft reservations
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION release_expired_reservations()
RETURNS INTEGER AS $$
DECLARE
    released_count INTEGER;
BEGIN
    -- Update inventory positions
    UPDATE inventory_positions ip
    SET quantity_reserved = quantity_reserved - expired.total_qty
    FROM (
        SELECT inventory_position_id, SUM(quantity_reserved) as total_qty
        FROM material_reservations
        WHERE reservation_type = 'SOFT'
          AND expires_at < NOW()
          AND fulfilled_at IS NULL
        GROUP BY inventory_position_id
    ) expired
    WHERE ip.id = expired.inventory_position_id;
    
    -- Delete expired reservations
    DELETE FROM material_reservations
    WHERE reservation_type = 'SOFT'
      AND expires_at < NOW()
      AND fulfilled_at IS NULL;
    
    GET DIAGNOSTICS released_count = ROW_COUNT;
    RETURN released_count;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

-- Update train target mileage for fleet balancing
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION update_fleet_mileage_targets()
RETURNS void AS $$
BEGIN
    UPDATE trains t
    SET target_mileage_km = fleet_avg.avg_mileage
    FROM (
        SELECT fleet_id, AVG(current_mileage_km)::INTEGER as avg_mileage
        FROM trains
        WHERE active = TRUE
        GROUP BY fleet_id
    ) fleet_avg
    WHERE t.fleet_id = fleet_avg.fleet_id
      AND t.active = TRUE;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
```

/**
 * MMS Database Enums
 *
 * PostgreSQL enum types for the Metro Fleet Mileage Management System.
 * These enums ensure data integrity and provide type safety across the application.
 *
 * @see specs/003-database-schema.md Section 3.2
 */

import { pgEnum } from 'drizzle-orm/pg-core';

/**
 * Train fitness status - determines operational capability
 * - FIT: Train is fully operational
 * - RESTRICTED: Train has limited capability (e.g., reduced speed)
 * - GROUNDED: Train cannot operate and is awaiting maintenance
 */
export const fitnessStatusEnum = pgEnum('fitness_status', ['FIT', 'RESTRICTED', 'GROUNDED']);

/**
 * PM schedule status - tracks maintenance task lifecycle
 * - SCHEDULED: PM is planned for a future date
 * - IN_PROGRESS: PM is currently being executed
 * - COMPLETED: PM has been finished successfully
 * - CANCELLED: PM was cancelled (with reason)
 * - DEFERRED: PM was postponed to a later date
 */
export const pmStatusEnum = pgEnum('pm_status', [
  'SCHEDULED',
  'IN_PROGRESS',
  'COMPLETED',
  'CANCELLED',
  'DEFERRED',
]);

/**
 * Track type - categorizes maintenance bay capability
 * - LIGHT: Light maintenance (C1)
 * - HEAVY: Heavy maintenance (C2, C3)
 * - OVERHAUL: Major overhaul (C4, OH)
 * - STABLING: Storage only, no maintenance capability
 */
export const trackTypeEnum = pgEnum('track_type', ['LIGHT', 'HEAVY', 'OVERHAUL', 'STABLING']);

/**
 * Work shift type - for man-hour planning
 */
export const shiftTypeEnum = pgEnum('shift_type', ['DAY', 'NIGHT', 'WEEKEND', 'HOLIDAY']);

/**
 * Man-hour adjustment status - workflow states
 */
export const adjustmentStatusEnum = pgEnum('adjustment_status', [
  'PROPOSED',
  'APPROVED',
  'REJECTED',
  'APPLIED',
]);

/**
 * Plan baseline status - snapshot lifecycle
 * - DRAFT: Being prepared, not yet official
 * - PUBLISHED: Active official baseline
 * - SUPERSEDED: Replaced by newer baseline
 * - ARCHIVED: Kept for historical reference
 */
export const baselineStatusEnum = pgEnum('baseline_status', [
  'DRAFT',
  'PUBLISHED',
  'SUPERSEDED',
  'ARCHIVED',
]);

/**
 * Scenario status - what-if analysis states
 * - ACTIVE: Being worked on
 * - PROMOTED: Accepted and converted to baseline
 * - DISCARDED: Rejected
 * - EXPIRED: Auto-expired after 30 days of inactivity
 */
export const scenarioStatusEnum = pgEnum('scenario_status', [
  'ACTIVE',
  'PROMOTED',
  'DISCARDED',
  'EXPIRED',
]);

/**
 * Material criticality - determines scheduling constraints
 * - BLOCKING: PM cannot start without this part
 * - DEFERRABLE: PM can start but needs part within X days
 * - CONSUMABLE: Nice-to-have, can substitute
 */
export const materialCriticalityEnum = pgEnum('material_criticality', [
  'BLOCKING',
  'DEFERRABLE',
  'CONSUMABLE',
]);

/**
 * Reservation type - material allocation commitment level
 * - SOFT: Tentative, can be released if PM moves
 * - HARD: Committed, parts allocated
 */
export const reservationTypeEnum = pgEnum('reservation_type', ['SOFT', 'HARD']);

/**
 * Day type - for service demand and utilization profiles (v2.1)
 */
export const dayTypeEnum = pgEnum('day_type', ['WEEKDAY', 'SATURDAY', 'SUNDAY', 'HOLIDAY']);

/**
 * Unavailability type - reason for train being out of service (v2.1)
 * - CORRECTIVE: Unplanned maintenance (from Maximo)
 * - INCIDENT: Safety incident requiring inspection
 * - COLLISION: Collision damage
 * - VANDALISM: Intentional damage
 * - EXTERNAL: External factors (weather, infrastructure)
 * - PLANNED_OUTAGE: Scheduled downtime
 */
export const unavailabilityTypeEnum = pgEnum('unavailability_type', [
  'CORRECTIVE',
  'INCIDENT',
  'COLLISION',
  'VANDALISM',
  'EXTERNAL',
  'PLANNED_OUTAGE',
]);

/**
 * Unavailability status (v2.1)
 */
export const unavailabilityStatusEnum = pgEnum('unavailability_status', ['ACTIVE', 'RESOLVED']);

/**
 * Track capability - specific equipment/features (v2.1)
 * Used for constraint-based PM scheduling
 */
export const trackCapabilityEnum = pgEnum('track_capability', [
  'PIT',
  'CRANE',
  'LIFTING_JACKS',
  'WHEEL_LATHE',
  'CATENARY_ISOLATION',
  'ROOF_ACCESS',
  'WASH',
  'DROP_TABLE',
]);

/**
 * Constraint type - technical compatibility constraints (v2.1)
 */
export const constraintTypeEnum = pgEnum('constraint_type', [
  'SIGNALING',
  'GAUGE',
  'POWER_SUPPLY',
  'PLATFORM_LENGTH',
  'MAX_SPEED',
  'TUNNEL_CLEARANCE',
]);

/**
 * Assumption type - for cold start data migration (v2.1)
 * Tracks how PM history was inferred when actual records are missing
 */
export const assumptionTypeEnum = pgEnum('assumption_type', [
  'INFERRED_FROM_COMMISSIONING',
  'INFERRED_FROM_HIGHER_PM',
  'MANUAL_OVERRIDE',
  'IMPORTED',
  'NO_HISTORY',
]);

/**
 * Confidence level - data quality indicator (v2.1)
 */
export const confidenceLevelEnum = pgEnum('confidence_level', [
  'HIGH',
  'MEDIUM',
  'LOW',
  'ESTIMATED',
]);

/**
 * Validation status - for mileage readings
 */
export const validationStatusEnum = pgEnum('validation_status', [
  'PENDING',
  'VALID',
  'ANOMALY',
  'CORRECTED',
]);

/**
 * Anomaly type - for mileage reading anomalies
 */
export const anomalyTypeEnum = pgEnum('anomaly_type', ['NEGATIVE', 'SPIKE', 'MISSING', 'DUPLICATE']);

/**
 * Meter event type - for odometer events
 */
export const meterEventTypeEnum = pgEnum('meter_event_type', [
  'REPLACEMENT',
  'RESET',
  'CALIBRATION',
  'SWAP',
]);

/**
 * Component status - train component lifecycle
 */
export const componentStatusEnum = pgEnum('component_status', [
  'IN_SERVICE',
  'SPARE',
  'IN_OVERHAUL',
  'CONDEMNED',
]);

/**
 * Removal reason - why a component was removed
 */
export const removalReasonEnum = pgEnum('removal_reason', [
  'SCHEDULED_OVERHAUL',
  'FAILURE',
  'SWAP',
  'UPGRADE',
]);

/**
 * Audit action - for audit log entries
 */
export const auditActionEnum = pgEnum('audit_action', [
  'CREATE',
  'UPDATE',
  'DELETE',
  'APPROVE',
  'REJECT',
  'PUBLISH',
]);

/**
 * Change source - who/what initiated a change
 */
export const changeSourceEnum = pgEnum('change_source', ['USER', 'OPTIMIZER', 'SYSTEM', 'IMPORT']);

/**
 * PM change type - specific PM schedule changes
 */
export const pmChangeTypeEnum = pgEnum('pm_change_type', [
  'CREATED',
  'DATE_CHANGED',
  'STATUS_CHANGED',
  'TRACK_ASSIGNED',
  'LOCKED',
  'UNLOCKED',
  'COMPLETED',
  'CANCELLED',
  'DEFERRED',
]);

/**
 * Maximo sync status - tracks shadow record synchronization state
 * - PENDING: Created in MMS, not yet synced to Maximo
 * - SYNCED: Successfully synchronized with Maximo
 * - CONFLICT: Conflict detected (Maximo changed after last sync)
 * - ERROR: Sync failed with error
 */
export const maximoSyncStatusEnum = pgEnum('maximo_sync_status', [
  'PENDING',
  'SYNCED',
  'CONFLICT',
  'ERROR',
]);

/**
 * Maximo work order status - mirrors Maximo status values
 * @see specs/007-integration.md Section 7.4.5
 */
export const maximoWoStatusEnum = pgEnum('maximo_wo_status', [
  'WAPPR',
  'APPR',
  'INPRG',
  'COMP',
  'CLOSE',
  'CAN',
]);

/**
 * Optimizer result status - indicates solution quality
 * - OPTIMAL: Best possible solution found
 * - FEASIBLE: Valid solution found (may not be optimal)
 * - FALLBACK: TypeScript fallback solver used (C++ unavailable)
 * - INFEASIBLE: No valid solution exists
 * @see specs/004-domain-services.md Section 4.3
 */
export const optimizerStatusEnum = pgEnum('optimizer_status', [
  'OPTIMAL',
  'FEASIBLE',
  'FALLBACK',
  'INFEASIBLE',
]);

1→/**
     2→ * MMS Core Fleet Domain Tables
     3→ *
     4→ * Asset master data: depots, fleets, trains, routes, and tracks.
     5→ * These form the foundation of the fleet management system.
     6→ *
     7→ * @see specs/003-database-schema.md Section 3.2
     8→ */
     9→
    10→import { relations } from 'drizzle-orm';
    11→import {
    12→  boolean,
    13→  date,
    14→  decimal,
    15→  index,
    16→  integer,
    17→  pgTable,
    18→  text,
    19→  timestamp,
    20→  uniqueIndex,
    21→  uuid,
    22→  varchar,
    23→} from 'drizzle-orm/pg-core';
    24→import { fitnessStatusEnum, trackTypeEnum } from './enums';
    25→
    26→/**
    27→ * Depots - Maintenance facilities
    28→ *
    29→ * Physical locations where trains are maintained and stored.
    30→ * Each depot has a timezone for correct scheduling calculations.
    31→ */
    32→export const depots = pgTable(
    33→  'depots',
    34→  {
    35→    id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
    36→    code: varchar('code', { length: 10 }).notNull().unique(),
    37→    name: varchar('name', { length: 100 }).notNull(),
    38→    location: varchar('location', { length: 200 }),
    39→    timezone: varchar('timezone', { length: 50 }).default('Asia/Singapore'),
    40→    active: boolean('active').default(true),
    41→    createdAt: timestamp('created_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
    42→    updatedAt: timestamp('updated_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
    43→  },
    44→  (table) => [uniqueIndex('idx_depots_code').on(table.code)]
    45→);
    46→
    47→/**
    48→ * Fleets - Train types/manufacturers
    49→ *
    50→ * A fleet represents a group of similar trains from the same manufacturer.
    51→ * PM types are defined per fleet as maintenance requirements vary by train model.
    52→ */
    53→export const fleets = pgTable(
    54→  'fleets',
    55→  {
    56→    id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
    57→    code: varchar('code', { length: 10 }).notNull().unique(),
    58→    name: varchar('name', { length: 100 }).notNull(),
    59→    manufacturer: varchar('manufacturer', { length: 100 }).notNull(),
    60→    model: varchar('model', { length: 100 }),
    61→    baseDepotId: uuid('base_depot_id').references(() => depots.id),

    70→/**
    71→ * Trains - Individual train assets

    81→export const trains = pgTable(
    82→  'trains',

1→/**
     2→ * MMS Operational Constraints Tables (v2.1)
     3→ *
     4→ * Critical tables for operational requirements and constraints:
     5→ * - Service demand: Minimum train availability (HARD constraint)
     6→ * - Route utilization profiles: Mileage projection accuracy
     7→ * - Unavailability events: CM/incident tracking (Maximo integration)
     8→ * - Train-route compatibility: Technical constraints
     9→ *
    10→ * @see specs/003-database-schema.md Sections 3.2.1 - 3.2.4
    11→ */
    12→
    13→import { relations, sql } from 'drizzle-orm';
    14→import {
    15→  boolean,
    16→  check,
    17→  date,
    18→  decimal,
    19→  index,
    20→  integer,
    21→  pgTable,
    22→  text,
    23→  timestamp,
    24→  uniqueIndex,
    25→  uuid,
    26→  varchar,
    27→} from 'drizzle-orm/pg-core';
    28→import { fleets, routes, trains } from './core-fleet';
    29→import {
    30→  confidenceLevelEnum,
    31→  constraintTypeEnum,
    32→  dayTypeEnum,
    33→  fitnessStatusEnum,
    34→  optimizerStatusEnum,
    35→  unavailabilityStatusEnum,
    36→  unavailabilityTypeEnum,
    37→} from './enums';
    38→import { users } from './users';

    40→/**
    41→ * Service Demand - Minimum train availability requirements
    42→ *
    43→ * This is a HARD CONSTRAINT for the optimizer - it cannot schedule
    44→ * maintenance that would violate availability targets.
    45→ *
    46→ * Service demand varies by:
    47→ * - Fleet (different fleets serve different lines)
    48→ * - Day type (weekday vs. weekend vs. holiday)
    49→ * - Time of day (peak AM/PM vs. midday vs. evening)
    50→ */
    51→export const serviceDemand = pgTable(
    52→  'service_demand',
    53→  {
    54→    id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
    55→    fleetId: uuid('fleet_id')
    56→      .notNull()
    57→      .references(() => fleets.id),
    58→    effectiveDate: date('effective_date').notNull(),
    59→    dayType: dayTypeEnum('day_type').notNull(),
    60→
    61→    // Minimum availability requirements
    62→    minTrainsRequired: integer('min_trains_required').notNull(),
    63→    peakAmRequirement: integer('peak_am_requirement'), // 6:00-9:00
    64→    middayRequirement: integer('midday_requirement'), // 9:00-16:00
    65→    peakPmRequirement: integer('peak_pm_requirement'), // 16:00-19:00
    66→    eveningRequirement: integer('evening_requirement'), // 19:00-23:00
    67→    standbyBuffer: integer('standby_buffer').default(2), // Additional contingency
    68→
    69→    // Metadata
    70→    source: varchar('source', { length: 50 }), // 'OPERATIONS', 'CONTRACT', 'MANUAL'
    71→    notes: text('notes'),
    72→
    73→    createdAt: timestamp('created_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
    74→    updatedAt: timestamp('updated_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
    75→    createdBy: uuid('created_by').references(() => users.id),
    76→  },
    77→  (table) => [
    78→    uniqueIndex('idx_service_demand_unique').on(table.fleetId, table.effectiveDate, table.dayType),
    79→    index('idx_service_demand_fleet').on(table.fleetId, table.effectiveDate),
    80→    index('idx_service_demand_lookup').on(table.fleetId, table.dayType, table.effectiveDate),
    81→  ]
    82→);
    83→
    84→/**
    85→ * Route Utilization Profiles - Expected daily mileage by route
    86→ *
    87→ * Enables accurate long-term mileage projections. Without route-specific
    88→ * profiles, projections use fleet-wide averages which can be off by
    89→ * 60,000+ km over a 12-year contract period.
    90→ *
    91→ * Key fields:
    92→ * - weekday_km, saturday_km, sunday_km: Daily mileage by day type
    93→ * - seasonal multipliers: Adjust for holiday periods
    94→ * - confidence: Data quality indicator
    95→ */
    96→export const routeUtilizationProfiles = pgTable(
    97→  'route_utilization_profiles',
    98→  {
    99→    id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
   100→    routeId: uuid('route_id')
   101→      .notNull()
   102→      .references(() => routes.id),
   103→    effectiveDate: date('effective_date').notNull().default(sql`CURRENT_DATE`),
   104→
   105→    // Daily mileage by day type (km)
   106→    weekdayKm: integer('weekday_km').notNull(),
   107→    saturdayKm: integer('saturday_km'),
   108→    sundayKm: integer('sunday_km'),
   109→    holidayKm: integer('holiday_km'),
   110→
   111→    // Seasonal adjustments (e.g., 1.15 for CNY peak)
   112→    peakSeasonMultiplier: decimal('peak_season_multiplier', { precision: 3, scale: 2 }).default(
   113→      '1.0'
   114→    ),
   115→    offPeakMultiplier: decimal('off_peak_multiplier', { precision: 3, scale: 2 }).default('1.0'),
   116→
   117→    // Service characteristics
   118→    serviceHoursPerDay: decimal('service_hours_per_day', { precision: 4, scale: 1 }),
   119→    roundTripKm: decimal('round_trip_km', { precision: 6, scale: 2 }),
   120→    tripsPerDay: integer('trips_per_day'),
   121→
   122→    // Metadata
   123→    source: varchar('source', { length: 50 }), // 'MEASURED', 'ESTIMATED', 'CONTRACT'
   124→    confidence: confidenceLevelEnum('confidence').default('MEDIUM'),
   125→    notes: text('notes'),
   126→
   127→    createdAt: timestamp('created_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
   128→    updatedAt: timestamp('updated_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
   129→  },
   130→  (table) => [
   131→    uniqueIndex('idx_route_util_unique').on(table.routeId, table.effectiveDate),
   132→    index('idx_route_util_route').on(table.routeId, table.effectiveDate),
   133→  ]
   134→);
   135→
   136→/**
   137→ * Unavailability Events - Trains out of service
   138→ *
   139→ * CRITICAL integration point with Maximo CMMS. Tracks trains that cannot
   140→ * be scheduled due to corrective maintenance, incidents, or other factors.
   141→ *
   142→ * Key behaviors:
   143→ * - When an event is created with status='ACTIVE', the train's fitness_status
   144→ *   is automatically updated via trigger
   145→ * - Events with restriction_level='GROUNDED' block both run assignment and PM
   146→ * - Events with restriction_level='RESTRICTED' may allow limited operations
   147→ *
   148→ * Maximo integration:
   149→ * - source_system='MAXIMO' for CM work orders
   150→ * - external_reference holds the Maximo WO number (e.g., 'WO-2026-001234')
   151→ * - external_status mirrors Maximo status (WAPPR, INPRG, COMP, etc.)
   152→ */
   153→export const unavailabilityEvents = pgTable(
   154→  'unavailability_events',
   155→  {
   156→    id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
   157→    trainId: uuid('train_id')
   158→      .notNull()
   159→      .references(() => trains.id),
   160→
   161→    // Event classification
   162→    eventType: unavailabilityTypeEnum('event_type').notNull(),
   163→    status: unavailabilityStatusEnum('status').notNull().default('ACTIVE'),
   164→    restrictionLevel: fitnessStatusEnum('restriction_level').notNull(), // 'RESTRICTED' or 'GROUNDED'
   165→
   166→    // Timing
   167→    reportedAt: timestamp('reported_at', { withTimezone: true }).notNull().defaultNow(),
   168→    estimatedRepairHours: integer('estimated_repair_hours'),
   169→    estimatedReturnDate: date('estimated_return_date'),
   170→    actualReturnDate: date('actual_return_date'),
   171→
   172→    // External system linkage (Maximo integration)
   173→    sourceSystem: varchar('source_system', { length: 30 }), // 'MAXIMO', 'OCC', 'MANUAL'
   174→    externalReference: varchar('external_reference', { length: 50 }), // Maximo WO number
   175→    externalStatus: varchar('external_status', { length: 30 }), // Maximo status
   176→
   177→    // Description
   178→    description: text('description'),
   179→    failureCode: varchar('failure_code', { length: 30 }), // Standard failure classification
   180→    affectedSystem: varchar('affected_system', { length: 50 }), // 'TRACTION', 'BRAKES', etc.
   181→
   182→    // Scheduling impact flags
   183→    blocksPmScheduling: boolean('blocks_pm_scheduling').default(true),
   184→    blocksRunAssignment: boolean('blocks_run_assignment').default(true),
   185→
   186→    // Resolution
   187→    resolvedAt: timestamp('resolved_at', { withTimezone: true }),
   188→    resolvedBy: uuid('resolved_by').references(() => users.id),
   189→    resolutionNotes: text('resolution_notes'),
   190→
   191→    createdAt: timestamp('created_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
   192→    updatedAt: timestamp('updated_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
   193→  },
   194→  (table) => [
   195→    // Constraint: Cannot have resolved data if status is ACTIVE
   196→    check(
   197→      'chk_resolved_status',
   198→      sql`(${table.status} = 'ACTIVE' AND ${table.resolvedAt} IS NULL) OR (${table.status} = 'RESOLVED' AND ${table.resolvedAt} IS NOT NULL)`
   199→    ),
   200→    // Primary query: Find active unavailability for a train
   201→    index('idx_unavailability_active').on(table.trainId, table.status),
   202→    // Dashboard query: All active unavailability by estimated return
   203→    index('idx_unavailability_return').on(table.estimatedReturnDate),
   204→    // Maximo sync: Find by external reference
   205→    index('idx_unavailability_external').on(table.sourceSystem, table.externalReference),
   206→    // Audit: Find by type and date range
   207→    index('idx_unavailability_audit').on(table.eventType, table.reportedAt),
   208→  ]
   209→);
   210→
   211→/**
   212→ * Train-Route Compatibility - Technical constraints
   213→ *
   214→ * Defines which trains can operate on which routes based on technical
   215→ * constraints (signaling, gauge, power supply, etc.). Prevents the
   216→ * optimizer from assigning incompatible trains to routes.
   217→ *
   218→ * Can be defined at:
   219→ * - Fleet level (fleet_id): Applies to all trains in the fleet
   220→ * - Train level (train_id): Overrides fleet-level for specific train
   221→ *
   222→ * Temporal support:
   223→ * - effective_date/expiry_date allow temporary restrictions
   224→ * - NULL expiry_date = permanent restriction
   225→ */
   226→export const trainRouteCompatibility = pgTable(
   227→  'train_route_compatibility',
   228→  {
   229→    id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
   230→
   231→    // Can be at train or fleet level (mutually exclusive)
   232→    trainId: uuid('train_id').references(() => trains.id),
   233→    fleetId: uuid('fleet_id').references(() => fleets.id),
   234→    routeId: uuid('route_id')
   235→      .notNull()
   236→      .references(() => routes.id),
   237→
   238→    // Compatibility determination
   239→    isCompatible: boolean('is_compatible').notNull().default(true),
   240→    constraintType: constraintTypeEnum('constraint_type'),
   241→    restrictionReason: text('restriction_reason'),
   242→
   243→    // Validity period (for temporary restrictions)
   244→    effectiveDate: date('effective_date').default(sql`CURRENT_DATE`),
   245→    expiryDate: date('expiry_date'), // NULL = permanent
   246→
   247→    // Metadata
   248→    verifiedBy: uuid('verified_by').references(() => users.id),
   249→    verifiedAt: timestamp('verified_at', { withTimezone: true }),
   250→    notes: text('notes'),
   251→
   252→    createdAt: timestamp('created_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
   253→    updatedAt: timestamp('updated_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
   254→  },
   255→  (table) => [
   256→    // Must specify either train_id OR fleet_id, not both
   257→    check(
   258→      'chk_train_or_fleet',
   259→      sql`(${table.trainId} IS NOT NULL AND ${table.fleetId} IS NULL) OR (${table.trainId} IS NULL AND ${table.fleetId} IS NOT NULL)`
   260→    ),
   261→    index('idx_compatibility_route').on(table.routeId, table.isCompatible),
   262→    index('idx_compatibility_train').on(table.trainId, table.isCompatible),
   263→    index('idx_compatibility_fleet').on(table.fleetId, table.isCompatible),
   264→    index('idx_compatibility_active').on(table.effectiveDate, table.expiryDate),
   265→  ]
   266→);

   320→/**
   321→ * Run Assignments - Daily train-to-route assignment history
   322→ *
   323→ * Stores the output of the run assignment optimizer for historical analysis.
   324→ * Used by RouteUtilizationService to determine typical route assignments for
   325→ * trains, enabling more accurate mileage projections.
   326→ *
   327→ * Key queries:
   328→ * - Find typical route for a train (most frequent in last 30 days)
   329→ * - Audit trail of train assignments
   330→ * - Service pattern analysis
   331→ *
   332→ * @see specs/004-domain-services.md Section 4.3
   333→ */
   334→export const runAssignments = pgTable(
   335→  'run_assignments',
   336→  {
   337→    id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
   338→    trainId: uuid('train_id')
   339→      .notNull()
   340→      .references(() => trains.id),
   341→    routeId: uuid('route_id')
   342→      .notNull()
   343→      .references(() => routes.id),
   344→    assignmentDate: date('assignment_date').notNull(),
   345→
   346→    // Optimizer run context
   347→    requestId: uuid('request_id').notNull(), // Groups assignments from same optimizer run
   348→    optimizerStatus: optimizerStatusEnum('optimizer_status').notNull(),
   349→    score: decimal('score', { precision: 10, scale: 4 }), // Assignment quality score
   350→
   351→    // Explanation data (stored as JSON for flexibility)
   352→    explanation: text('explanation'), // JSON array of ExplanationTrace
   353→
   354→    createdAt: timestamp('created_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
   355→  },
   356→  (table) => [
   357→    // Primary query: Find recent assignments for a train to determine typical route
   358→    index('idx_run_assignments_train_date').on(table.trainId, table.assignmentDate),
   359→    // Find all assignments for a specific date (for daily operations view)
   360→    index('idx_run_assignments_date').on(table.assignmentDate),
   361→    // Group assignments by optimizer run
   362→    index('idx_run_assignments_request').on(table.requestId),
   363→    // Route-centric queries (route utilization analysis)
   364→    index('idx_run_assignments_route_date').on(table.routeId, table.assignmentDate),
   365→    // Unique constraint: one assignment per train per day

1→/**
     2→ * MMS Track Capabilities Tables (v2.1)
     3→ *
     4→ * Track equipment/features and PM requirements for constraint-based scheduling.
     5→ * Also includes PM baseline assumptions for cold start data migration.
     6→ *
     7→ * @see specs/003-database-schema.md Sections 3.2.5 - 3.2.6
     8→ */
     9→
    10→import { relations } from 'drizzle-orm';
    11→import {
    12→  boolean,
    13→  date,
    14→  index,
    15→  integer,
    16→  jsonb,
    17→  pgTable,
    18→  text,
    19→  timestamp,
    20→  uniqueIndex,
    21→  uuid,
    22→} from 'drizzle-orm/pg-core';
    23→import { tracks, trains } from './core-fleet';
    24→import {
    25→  assumptionTypeEnum,
    26→  confidenceLevelEnum,
    27→  trackCapabilityEnum,
    28→} from './enums';
    29→import { pmTypes } from './pm-management';
    30→import { users } from './users';
    31→
    32→/**
    33→ * Track Capabilities - Equipment and features per track
    34→ *
    35→ * Defines specific equipment available on each maintenance track.
    36→ * Used to match PM requirements to capable tracks.
    37→ *
    38→ * Examples:
    39→ * - Track T1 at Bishan has: PIT, CRANE, LIFTING_JACKS
    40→ * - Track T5 at Changi has: WHEEL_LATHE, DROP_TABLE
    41→ */
    42→export const trackCapabilities = pgTable(
    43→  'track_capabilities',
    44→  {
    45→    id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
    46→    trackId: uuid('track_id')
    47→      .notNull()
    48→      .references(() => tracks.id),
    49→    capability: trackCapabilityEnum('capability').notNull(),
    50→
    51→    // Capacity limits for this capability (e.g., max lift weight)
    52→    capacityLimit: jsonb('capacity_limit'), // {"max_axle_load_tonnes": 17, "max_lift_tonnes": 25}
    53→
    54→    // Availability
    55→    isOperational: boolean('is_operational').default(true),
    56→    maintenanceSchedule: text('maintenance_schedule'), // "Unavailable Tuesdays 06:00-10:00"
    57→
    58→    createdAt: timestamp('created_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
    59→  },
    60→  (table) => [
    61→    uniqueIndex('idx_track_cap_unique').on(table.trackId, table.capability),
    62→    index('idx_track_cap_track').on(table.trackId),
    63→    index('idx_track_cap_type').on(table.capability),
    64→  ]
    65→);
    66→
    67→/**
    68→ * PM Capability Requirements - What capabilities each PM type needs
    69→ *
    70→ * Links PM types to required track capabilities.
    71→ * Mandatory vs. preferred:
    72→ * - is_mandatory=true: PM cannot proceed without this capability
    73→ * - is_mandatory=false: Preferred but can use alternative
    74→ *
    75→ * Example: C4 overhaul requires PIT (mandatory), LIFTING_JACKS (mandatory), CRANE (preferred)
    76→ */
    77→export const pmCapabilityRequirements = pgTable(
    78→  'pm_capability_requirements',
    79→  {
    80→    id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
    81→    pmTypeId: uuid('pm_type_id')
    82→      .notNull()
    83→      .references(() => pmTypes.id),
    84→    capability: trackCapabilityEnum('capability').notNull(),
    85→    isMandatory: boolean('is_mandatory').default(true),
    86→
    87→    notes: text('notes'), // Why this capability is needed
    88→
    89→    createdAt: timestamp('created_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
    90→  },
    91→  (table) => [
    92→    uniqueIndex('idx_pm_cap_req_unique').on(table.pmTypeId, table.capability),
    93→    index('idx_pm_cap_req_pm').on(table.pmTypeId),
    94→    index('idx_pm_cap_req_cap').on(table.capability),
    95→  ]
    96→);
    97→
    98→/**
    99→ * PM Baseline Assumptions - Cold start data migration
   100→ *
   101→ * Handles scenarios where PM history is incomplete during data migration.
   102→ * Tracks how PM dates/mileage were inferred with confidence levels.
   103→ *
   104→ * Inference rules (implemented in application logic):
   105→ * 1. INFERRED_FROM_COMMISSIONING: If train commissioned 2020-01-01 and C1 interval
   106→ *    is 6 months, assume first PM at 2020-07-01
   107→ * 2. INFERRED_FROM_HIGHER_PM: If C4 completed 2024-06-15, C1/C2/C3 counters reset
   108→ *    from that date
   109→ * 3. NO_HISTORY: No data, use commissioning date with requires_review=true
   110→ *
   111→ * Records with LOW/ESTIMATED confidence should have requires_review=true
   112→ * for manual verification by operators.
   113→ */
   114→export const pmBaselineAssumptions = pgTable(
   115→  'pm_baseline_assumptions',
   116→  {
   117→    id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
   118→    trainId: uuid('train_id')
   119→      .notNull()
   120→      .references(() => trains.id),
   121→    pmTypeId: uuid('pm_type_id')
   122→      .notNull()
   123→      .references(() => pmTypes.id),
   124→
   125→    // Assumption details
   126→    assumptionType: assumptionTypeEnum('assumption_type').notNull(),
   127→
   128→    // Assumed baseline values
   129→    assumedLastPmDate: date('assumed_last_pm_date'),
   130→    assumedLastPmMileage: integer('assumed_last_pm_mileage'),
   131→    confidenceLevel: confidenceLevelEnum('confidence_level').notNull(),
   132→
   133→    // Inference source documentation
   134→    inferenceSource: text('inference_source'), // How assumption was derived
   135→    inferenceData: jsonb('inference_data'), // Supporting data
   136→
   137→    // Review workflow
   138→    requiresReview: boolean('requires_review').default(false),
   139→    reviewedBy: uuid('reviewed_by').references(() => users.id),
   140→    reviewedAt: timestamp('reviewed_at', { withTimezone: true }),
   141→    reviewNotes: text('review_notes'),
   142→
   143→    // When applied to the system
   144→    appliedAt: timestamp('applied_at', { withTimezone: true }),
   145→    appliedBy: uuid('applied_by').references(() => users.id),
   146→
   147→    createdAt: timestamp('created_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
   148→    updatedAt: timestamp('updated_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow(),
   149→  },
   150→  (table) => [
   151→    uniqueIndex('idx_assumptions_unique').on(table.trainId, table.pmTypeId),
   152→    index('idx_assumptions_train').on(table.trainId),
   153→    index('idx_assumptions_review').on(table.requiresReview),
   154→    index('idx_assumptions_confidence').on(table.confidenceLevel),
   155→  ]
   156→);
   157→
   158→// ============================================================================
   159→// Relations
   160→// ============================================================================
   161→
   162→export const trackCapabilitiesRelations = relations(trackCapabilities, ({ one }) => ({
   163→  track: one(tracks, {
   164→    fields: [trackCapabilities.trackId],
   165→    references: [tracks.id],
   166→  }),
   167→}));
   168→
   169→export const pmCapabilityRequirementsRelations = relations(pmCapabilityRequirements, ({ one }) => ({
   170→  pmType: one(pmTypes, {
   171→    fields: [pmCapabilityRequirements.pmTypeId],
   172→    references: [pmTypes.id],
   173→  }),
   174→}));
   175→
   176→export const pmBaselineAssumptionsRelations = relations(pmBaselineAssumptions, ({ one }) => ({
   177→  train: one(trains, {
   178→    fields: [pmBaselineAssumptions.trainId],
   179→    references: [trains.id],
   180→  }),
   181→  pmType: one(pmTypes, {
   182→    fields: [pmBaselineAssumptions.pmTypeId],
   183→    references: [pmTypes.id],
   184→  }),
   185→  reviewedByUser: one(users, {
   186→    fields: [pmBaselineAssumptions.reviewedBy],
   187→    references: [users.id],
   188→    relationName: 'reviewedBy',
   189→  }),
   190→  appliedByUser: one(users, {
   191→    fields: [pmBaselineAssumptions.appliedBy],
   192→    references: [users.id],
   193→    relationName: 'appliedBy',
   194→  }),
   195→}));
